papers.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
papers.abstract: Abstract EvoArena benchmark and EvoMem memory paradigm address the challenge of dynamic environments in LLM agents by modeling progressive updates and structured memory evolution, showing improved performance on evolving tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large language model (LLM) agents have achieved strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, yet most evaluations assume static environments. In contrast, real-world deployment is inherently dynamic, requiring agents to continually align their knowledge, skills, and behavior with changing environments and updated task conditions. To address this gap, we introduce EvoArena, a benchmark suite that models environment changes as sequences of progressive updates across terminal, software, and social domains. We further propose EvoMem, a patch-based memory paradigm that records memory evolution as structured update histories, enabling agents to reason about environmental evolution through changes in their memory. Experiments show that current agents struggle on EvoArena, achieving an average accuracy of 39.6% across evolving terminal, software, and social-preference domains. EvoMem consistently improves performance, yielding an average gain of 1.5% on EvoArena and also improving standard benchmarks such as GAIA and LoCoMo by 6.1% and 4.8%. Beyond individual tasks, EvoMem further improves chain-level accuracy by 3.7% on EvoArena, where success requires completing a consecutive sequence of related evolutionary subtasks. Mechanistic analysis shows that EvoMem improves evidence capture in the memory, indicating better preservation of complete evolving environment states. Our results highlight the importance of modeling evolution in both evaluation and memory for reliable agent deployment.

ru.title: Память, которая растет вместе с миром: агенты для динамических сред
ru.desc: В статье представлены EvoArena — бенчмарк для оценки LLM агентов в динамических окружениях, и EvoMem — парадигма структурированной памяти, которая отслеживает эволюцию среды через историю обновлений. Авторы показывают, что современные агенты плохо справляются с эволюционирующими задачами (39.6% точность), но предложенный подход с памятью на основе патчей улучшает производительность на 1.5-6.1% в зависимости от задачи. EvoMem позволяет агентам лучше рассуждать об изменениях окружения, сохраняя полное состояние эволюционирующей среды в памяти. Исследование демонстрирует критическую важность моделирования эволюции как при оценке агентов, так и при проектировании их систем памяти для надежного развертывания.

en.title: Adapting LLMs to Evolving Environments with EvoArena and EvoMem
en.desc: The paper introduces EvoArena, a benchmark designed to evaluate large language model (LLM) agents in dynamic environments, where tasks evolve over time. It also presents EvoMem, a memory paradigm that tracks changes in memory as structured updates, allowing agents to adapt to these evolving conditions. Experiments reveal that traditional LLM agents perform poorly on EvoArena, but incorporating EvoMem leads to improved accuracy across various tasks. This research emphasizes the necessity of considering environmental evolution in both the assessment and memory management of LLM agents for effective real-world applications.

zh.title: 动态环境中的智能代理演变
zh.desc: 本论文提出了EvoArena基准和EvoMem记忆范式，以应对大语言模型（LLM）代理在动态环境中的挑战。EvoArena通过将环境变化建模为逐步更新的序列，帮助代理在不断变化的任务条件下保持知识和技能的对齐。EvoMem则记录记忆演变的结构化更新历史，使代理能够通过记忆中的变化推理环境的演变。实验结果表明，EvoMem显著提高了代理在EvoArena上的表现，强调了在评估和记忆中建模演变的重要性。

papers.title: WeaveBench: A Long-Horizon, Real-World Benchmark for Computer-Use Agents with Hybrid Interfaces
papers.abstract: Abstract WeaveBench presents a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating computer-use agents across multiple interfaces, revealing significant challenges in long-horizon task orchestration and highlighting the limitations of traditional performance assessment methods.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Computer-use agents (CUAs) increasingly operate in runtimes that combine visual desktop control, command-line execution, code editing, browsers, and external tools. Existing benchmarks, however, often evaluate these interfaces as separable capabilities, leaving long-horizon cross-interface orchestration under-tested. Thus, we introduce WeaveBench, a long-horizon hybrid-interface benchmark with 114 tasks across 8 real-world work domains, grounded in real user requests and publicly verifiable artifacts. Each task requires agents to combine GUI observations/actions with CLI/code operations within a single trajectory. We evaluate these tasks on a real Ubuntu desktop inside deployed CLI-agent runtimes, augmented with a minimal desktop-control plugin. We also propose a companion trajectory-aware judge that inspects deliverables, files, screenshots, logs, and action traces, while detecting shortcut behaviors such as fabricated visual evidence or hard-coded metrics. Across frontier model-runtime pairings, the best PassRate reaches only 41.2%, showing the benchmark remains far from saturated. The trajectory-aware judge further reveals that outcome-only grading substantially overestimates agent performance. Overall, WeaveBench exposes a critical gap in CUA evaluation and provides an effective testbed to measure whether agents can orchestrate GUI, CLI, and code operations across long-horizon real-world tasks.

ru.title: От изолированных навыков к оркестрации: эволюция оценки агентов компьютерного управления
ru.desc: WeaveBench представляет комплексный бенчмарк для оценки агентов компьютерного управления, которые взаимодействуют с несколькими интерфейсами одновременно. В отличие от существующих методов, бенчмарк фокусируется на долгогоризонтных задачах, требующих координации между графическим интерфейсом, командной строкой и операциями с кодом в едином траектории агента. Исследователи предложили специализированный оценщик, осведомленный о траектории выполнения, который анализирует артефакты и выявляет манипуляции вроде поддельных визуальных доказательств. Результаты показывают, что даже лучшие модели достигают только 41,2% успешности, указывая на значительный разрыв между текущими возможностями агентов и требуемым уровнем производительности.

en.title: WeaveBench: Bridging the Gap in CUA Evaluation
en.desc: WeaveBench is a new benchmark designed to evaluate computer-use agents (CUAs) that perform tasks across different interfaces like graphical user interfaces (GUIs) and command-line interfaces (CLIs). It addresses the challenge of long-horizon task orchestration, where agents must seamlessly integrate various operations over extended periods. The benchmark includes 114 tasks from real-world scenarios, requiring agents to combine visual and code-based actions in a single workflow. Additionally, a trajectory-aware judging system is introduced to provide a more accurate assessment of agent performance, revealing that traditional evaluation methods may overestimate their capabilities.

zh.title: WeaveBench：评估计算机使用代理的新基准
zh.desc: WeaveBench是一个全面的基准测试，用于评估计算机使用代理在多个接口上的表现。它揭示了在长时间任务协调中存在的重大挑战，并强调了传统性能评估方法的局限性。该基准测试包含114个任务，涵盖8个真实工作领域，要求代理在单一轨迹中结合图形用户界面（GUI）和命令行界面（CLI）的操作。WeaveBench为评估计算机使用代理的能力提供了一个有效的平台，特别是在长时间的真实世界任务中。

papers.title: MiniMax Sparse Attention
papers.abstract: Abstract MiniMax Sparse Attention enables efficient processing of ultra-long contexts in large language models through blockwise sparsity and optimized GPU execution, achieving significant speedups while maintaining performance.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Ultra-long-context capability is becoming indispensable for frontier LLMs: agentic workflows, repository-scale code reasoning, and persistent memory all require the model to jointly attend over hundreds of thousands to millions of tokens, yet the quadratic cost of softmax attention makes this untenable at deployment scale. We introduce MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA), a blockwise sparse attention built upon Grouped Query Attention (GQA). A lightweight Index Branch scores key-value blocks and independently selects a Top-k subset for each GQA group, enabling group-specific sparse retrieval while maintaining efficient block-level execution; the Main Branch then performs exact block-sparse attention over only the selected blocks. Designed around a principle of simplicity and scalability, MSA is deliberately streamlined, making it straightforward to deploy efficiently across a broad range of GPUs. To translate sparsity into practical speedups, we co-design MSA with a GPU execution path that uses exp-free Top-k selection and KV-outer sparse attention to improve tensor-core utilization under block-granular access. On a 109B-parameter model with native multimodal training, MSA performs on par with GQA while reducing per-token attention compute by 28.4x at 1M context. Paired with our co-designed kernel, MSA achieves 14.2x prefill and 7.6x decoding wall-clock speedups on H800. Our inference kernel is available at: https://github.com/MiniMax-AI/MSA. A production-grade natively multimodal model powered by MSA has been publicly released at: https://huggingface.co/MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M3.
affiliations: Huazhong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Блочная разреженность для молниеносной обработки миллионов токенов
ru.desc: В статье представляется MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) — эффективный метод обработки сверхдлинных контекстов в больших языковых моделях через блочную разреженность внимания. Метод основан на Grouped Query Attention и использует лёгкий Index Branch для выбора Top-k наиболее релевантных блоков ключей и значений для каждой группы, что позволяет сохранить производительность модели. Авторы разработали оптимизированный GPU kernel с использованием экспоненциально-свободного Top-k отбора и KV-outer sparse attention для максимального использования тензорных ядер. На модели с 109 миллиардами параметров MSA достигает 28.4x сокращения вычислений внимания на контексте в 1 миллион токенов и ускорения 14.2x на этапе prefill и 7.6x на этапе декодирования на GPU H800.

en.title: Efficient Long-Context Processing with MiniMax Sparse Attention
en.desc: MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) is a new method designed to improve the efficiency of large language models (LLMs) when processing very long texts. It uses a blockwise sparse attention mechanism that allows the model to focus on only the most relevant parts of the input, significantly reducing the computational cost associated with traditional attention methods. By implementing a lightweight Index Branch for selecting key-value blocks and a Main Branch for executing attention on these blocks, MSA achieves faster processing speeds while maintaining high performance. This approach not only enhances the model's ability to handle ultra-long contexts but also optimizes GPU usage, resulting in substantial speed improvements during inference.

zh.title: 高效处理超长上下文的稀疏注意力
zh.desc: MiniMax稀疏注意力（MSA）通过块状稀疏性和优化的GPU执行，能够高效处理超长上下文，显著提高速度同时保持性能。该方法基于分组查询注意力（GQA），采用轻量级索引分支对关键值块进行评分，并为每个GQA组独立选择Top-k子集，实现组特定的稀疏检索。MSA设计简洁且可扩展，便于在多种GPU上高效部署，并通过与GPU执行路径的共同设计，提升了张量核心的利用率。最终，MSA在109B参数模型上实现了每个token计算减少28.4倍的效果，同时在H800上实现了14.2倍的预填充和7.6倍的解码速度提升。

papers.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract SpatialClaw is a training-free framework that uses code as an action interface to enable flexible, stateful spatial reasoning in vision-language models, achieving superior performance across diverse 3D/4D spatial reasoning tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Spatial reasoning, the ability to determine where objects are, how they relate, and how they move in 3D, remains a fundamental challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). Tool-augmented agents attempt to address this by augmenting VLMs with specialist perception modules, yet their effectiveness is bounded by the action interface through which those tools are invoked. In this work, we study how the design of this interface shapes the agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning. Existing spatial agents either employ single-pass code execution, which commits to a full analysis strategy before any intermediate result is observed, or rely on a structured tool-call interface that often offers less flexibility for freely composing operations or tailoring the analysis to each task. Both designs offer limited flexibility for open-ended, complex 3D/4D spatial reasoning. We therefore propose SpatialClaw, a training-free framework for spatial reasoning that adopts code as the action interface. SpatialClaw maintains a stateful Python kernel pre-loaded with input frames and a suite of perception and geometry primitives, letting a VLM-backed agent write one executable cell per step conditioned on all prior outputs, enabling the agent to flexibly compose and manipulate perception results and adapt its analysis to both intermediate text and visual observations and the demands of each problem. Evaluated across 20 spatial reasoning benchmarks spanning a broad range of static and dynamic 3D/4D spatial reasoning tasks, SpatialClaw achieves 59.9% average accuracy, outperforming the recent spatial agent by +11.2 points, with consistent gains across six VLM backbones from two model families without any benchmark- or model-specific adaptation.

ru.title: Код как рассуждение о пространстве: гибкий агент для 3D анализа
ru.desc: SpatialClaw — это инновационный фреймворк, который использует код в качестве интерфейса для действий, позволяя видео-языковым моделям гибко решать задачи пространственного рассуждения в 3D и 4D пространстве. В отличие от существующих подходов, которые либо выполняют весь анализ за один раз, либо ограничены структурированным интерфейсом, SpatialClaw поддерживает состояние через Python ядро с набором инструментов восприятия и геометрических примитивов. Агент может писать и выполнять код пошагово, адаптируя стратегию анализа на основе промежуточных результатов, что обеспечивает значительно большую гибкость при работе со сложными задачами. Модель достигла среднюю точность 59,9% на 20 бенчмарках пространственного рассуждения, превзойдя предыдущие методы на 11,2 процентных пункта, демонстрируя универсальность без специализации под конкретные наборы данных.

en.title: Empowering Vision-Language Models with Flexible Spatial Reasoning
en.desc: SpatialClaw is a novel framework designed for spatial reasoning in vision-language models (VLMs) that does not require training. It utilizes code as an action interface, allowing for flexible and stateful interactions with spatial data. This approach enables agents to dynamically compose and manipulate perception results based on previous outputs, enhancing their ability to tackle complex 3D/4D reasoning tasks. The framework has demonstrated superior performance across various benchmarks, achieving a significant accuracy improvement over existing spatial agents.

zh.title: SpatialClaw：灵活的空间推理框架
zh.desc: SpatialClaw 是一个无训练的框架，利用代码作为动作接口，使视觉语言模型能够灵活地进行状态感知的空间推理。空间推理是指确定物体的位置、关系和运动方式的能力，这对视觉语言模型来说仍然是一个基本挑战。现有的空间代理通常采用单次代码执行或结构化工具调用接口，限制了其在复杂空间推理中的灵活性。SpatialClaw 通过维护一个状态化的 Python 内核，允许代理根据先前的输出灵活地组合和操作感知结果，从而在多种空间推理任务中表现出色。

papers.title: InterleaveThinker: Reinforcing Agentic Interleaved Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract InterleaveThinker enables interleaved generation capabilities for image generators through a multi-agent pipeline with planner and critic agents, achieving performance comparable to state-of-the-art models while enhancing reasoning benchmarks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Recent image generators have demonstrated impressive photorealism and instruction-following capabilities in single-image generation and editing. However, constrained by their architectures, they cannot achieve interleaved generation (text-image sequence), which has crucial applications in visual narratives, guidance, and embodied manipulation. Even the latest open-source Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) exhibit limited performance in this regard. In this paper, we introduce InterleaveThinker, the first multi-agent pipeline designed to endow any existing image generator with interleaved generation capabilities. Specifically, we employ a planner agent to organize the image-text input sequence, instructing the image generator on the required execution at each step. Subsequently, we introduce a critic agent to evaluate the generator's outputs, identify samples that deviate from the planned instructions, and refine the instructions for regeneration. To implement this pipeline, we construct the Interleave-Planner-SFT-80k and Interleave-Critic-SFT-112k to perform a format cold-start. Then we develop Interleave-Critic-RL-13k to reinforce the step-wise instruction correction capability within a generation trajectory using GRPO. Since a single interleaved generation trajectory may involve over 25 generator calls, optimizing the entire trajectory is computationally impractical. Therefore, we propose accuracy reward and step-wise reward, allowing single-step RL to effectively guide the entire generation trajectory. The results show that InterleaveThinker improves performance across various image generators. On interleaved generation benchmarks, it achieves performance comparable to Nano Banana and GPT-5. Surprisingly, it also significantly enhances the base model on reasoning-based benchmarks; for example, on 4-step FLUX.2-klein, we observe substantial gains on WISE and RISE.

ru.title: Чередующееся поколение изображений через многоагентное взаимодействие планировщика и критика
ru.desc: InterleaveThinker — это многоагентный конвейер, который добавляет возможность чередующегося поколения (последовательности текста и изображений) к существующим генераторам изображений. Система использует агента-планировщика для организации последовательности входных данных и агента-критика для оценки выходов и уточнения инструкций. Для обучения применяются два набора данных с инструкциями (SFT) и укрепление с помощью GRPO с новыми метриками вознаграждения, позволяющими эффективно оптимизировать всю траекторию генерации. Результаты демонстрируют, что InterleaveThinker достигает производительности, сравнимой с современными моделями, и улучшает показатели на бенчмарках рассуждения.

en.title: InterleaveThinker: Revolutionizing Image Generation with Multi-Agent Interleaving
en.desc: InterleaveThinker is a novel multi-agent system that enhances image generation by enabling interleaved generation, which combines text and image outputs in a sequence. It utilizes a planner agent to structure the input sequence and guide the image generator, while a critic agent evaluates the outputs and refines instructions for better results. This approach allows existing image generators to perform better in tasks requiring visual narratives and complex interactions. The system demonstrates competitive performance against leading models and shows significant improvements in reasoning tasks, indicating its versatility and effectiveness in various applications.

zh.title: 交错生成，图像生成的新突破
zh.desc: InterleaveThinker 是一种多代理管道，能够为图像生成器提供交错生成能力。通过使用规划者和评论者代理，它在生成图像时能够更好地处理文本与图像的顺序关系。该方法不仅提升了现有图像生成模型的性能，还在推理基准测试中取得了显著进展。实验结果表明，InterleaveThinker 在交错生成基准上表现出色，达到了与最先进模型相当的水平。

papers.title: Robust-U1: Can MLLMs Self-Recover Corrupted Visual Content for Robust Understanding?
papers.abstract: Abstract Robust-U1 enhances multimodal large language models' robustness against visual corruptions through self-recovery capabilities that improve both visual quality and reasoning performance.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have demonstrated remarkable success in visual understanding, yet their performance degrades significantly under real-world visual corruptions. While existing robustness enhancement approaches exist, they are limited: black-box feature alignment lacks interpretability, and white-box text-based reasoning cannot restore lost pixel-level details. This work investigates a fundamental research question: Can MLLMs recover corrupted visual content by themselves? To address this, we propose Robust-U1, a novel framework that equips MLLMs with explicit visual self-recovery capability for robust understanding. The approach comprises three core stages: supervised fine-tuning for initial reconstruction, reinforcement learning with dual rewards (pixel-level SSIM and semantic-level CLIP similarity) for aligning high visual quality, and multimodal reasoning that jointly considers both the corrupted input and the recovered image. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Robust-U1 achieves state-of-the-art robustness on the real-world corruption benchmark and maintains superior performance under adversarial corruptions on general VQA benchmarks. Analysis confirms that high-quality visual recovery directly enhances reasoning performance, establishing self-recovery as a critical mechanism for robust visual understanding. The source code is available at https://github.com/jqtangust/Robust-U1.
affiliations: Nanjing University of Science and Technology, Nanjing, China
The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou), Guangzhou, China
The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, Hong Kong

ru.title: Самовосстановление изображений для надёжного понимания в мультимодальных моделях
ru.desc: Robust-U1 — это фреймворк, который дополняет мультимодальные большие языковые модели (MLLM) способностью к самовосстановлению повреждённых изображений для повышения их робастности. Авторы предлагают трёхэтапный подход: первоначальное обучение на размеченных данных для восстановления пикселей, обучение с подкреплением с двойными вознаграждениями (SSIM и CLIP similarity) для выравнивания качества, и совместное многомодальное рассуждение с использованием как повреждённого, так и восстановленного изображения. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что предложенный метод достигает стабильной производительности на эталонных наборах данных с реальными артефактами и состязательными помехами. Исследование демонстрирует, что высокое качество визуального восстановления напрямую улучшает производительность модели в задачах визуального понимания.

en.title: Empowering MLLMs with Self-Recovery for Robust Visual Understanding
en.desc: Robust-U1 is a framework designed to improve the robustness of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) against visual corruptions. It introduces a self-recovery capability that allows these models to reconstruct corrupted visual content, enhancing both visual quality and reasoning performance. The framework operates in three stages: initial reconstruction through supervised fine-tuning, reinforcement learning for optimizing visual quality, and multimodal reasoning that integrates both the corrupted input and the recovered image. Experimental results show that Robust-U1 outperforms existing methods in handling real-world visual corruptions and adversarial scenarios, highlighting the importance of self-recovery in robust visual understanding.

zh.title: 自我恢复，提升视觉理解的鲁棒性
zh.desc: Robust-U1 是一种增强多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）对视觉损坏的鲁棒性的新框架。它通过自我恢复能力来改善视觉质量和推理性能，解决了现有方法的局限性。该方法包括三个核心阶段：监督微调、双重奖励的强化学习和多模态推理。实验表明，Robust-U1 在真实世界的损坏基准测试中表现出色，证明了高质量的视觉恢复直接提升了推理性能。

papers.title: MaxProof: Scaling Mathematical Proof with Generative-Verifier RL and Population-Level Test-Time Scaling
papers.abstract: Abstract MaxProof is a test-time scaling framework that enhances mathematical proof generation by combining multiple proof-oriented capabilities and using population-level search with tournament selection to achieve competitive performance on high-level mathematical competitions.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We present MaxProof, a population-level test-time scaling framework for competition-level mathematical proof in the MiniMax-M3 series. M3 first trains three proof-oriented capabilities -- proof generation, proof verification, and critique-conditioned proof repair -- using a defense-in-depth generative verifier engineered for low false-positive rate. These capabilities are merged into a single released M3 model. At test time, MaxProof treats the model as a generator, verifier, refiner, and ranker, searches over a population of candidate proofs, and returns one final proof through tournament selection. With MaxProof test-time scaling, the M3 model reaches 35/42 on IMO 2025 and 36/42 on USAMO 2026, exceeding the human gold-medal threshold on both.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Турнирный отбор доказательств через популяционный поиск на этапе тестирования
ru.desc: В работе представлен MaxProof — фреймворк масштабирования во время тестирования, который улучшает генерацию математических доказательств путём объединения нескольких специализированных возможностей: генерации доказательств, их верификации и критическо-обусловленного восстановления. Модель M3 обучается с использованием защиты в глубину на основе генеративного верификатора с низким уровнем ложных срабатываний. Во время тестирования MaxProof применяет популяционный поиск с турнирной селекцией для выбора наилучшего доказательства из множества кандидатов. Достигнутые результаты превышают порог золотой медали на олимпиадах IMO 2025 и USAMO 2026.

en.title: MaxProof: Elevating Mathematical Proofs with Competitive Intelligence
en.desc: MaxProof is a framework designed to improve the generation of mathematical proofs during testing. It integrates three key capabilities: generating proofs, verifying them, and repairing them based on critiques. By employing a population-level search strategy with tournament selection, MaxProof efficiently identifies the best proof from a set of candidates. This approach has led to impressive results in high-level mathematical competitions, surpassing human performance benchmarks.

zh.title: MaxProof：提升数学证明生成的竞争力
zh.desc: MaxProof 是一个测试时扩展框架，旨在通过结合多种证明能力来增强数学证明生成。它使用种群级搜索和锦标赛选择方法，在高水平数学竞赛中实现竞争力表现。M3 模型首先训练了三种与证明相关的能力：证明生成、证明验证和基于批评的证明修复。测试时，MaxProof 将模型视为生成器、验证器、精炼器和排名器，搜索候选证明并通过锦标赛选择返回最终证明。

papers.title: FORT-Searcher: Synthesizing Shortcut-Resistant Search Tasks for Training Deep Search Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract A framework for creating shortcut-resistant training data for deep search agents by identifying and mitigating four shortcut risks in data synthesis processes.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Training deep search agents requires verifiable questions whose answers remain unavailable until sufficient evidence has been acquired through search. Existing synthesis methods often increase apparent difficulty by enriching graph structures, but structural complexity alone does not guarantee realized search difficulty: the intended search process can collapse through a cheaper identifying route. We formalize this gap with a shortcut-aware difficulty framework and identify four actionable shortcut risks: evidence co-coverage, single-clue selectivity, exposed constants, and prior-knowledge binding. To diagnose their realized effects, we use trajectory signatures including solving cost, answer hit time, and prior-shortcut rate. Guided by this framework, we introduce FORT, a Framework of Shortcut-Resistant Training-Data Synthesis. FORT constructs shortcut-resistant training data by controlling shortcut risks across entity selection, evidence graph construction, question formulation, and adversarial refinement. Experiments show that FORT induces longer pre-answer search and fewer shortcut patterns than existing open-source deep search datasets. Using the resulting trajectories, we train FORT-Searcher with supervised fine-tuning (SFT) only, and it achieves the best overall performance among comparable-size open-source search agents on challenging deep search benchmarks. Relevant resources will be made available at https://github.com/RUCAIBox/FORT-Searcher.
affiliations: Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence, Renmin University of China

ru.title: Блокируем ярлычки: устойчивый синтез данных для глубокого поиска
ru.desc: В статье предлагается фреймворк FORT для создания обучающих данных, устойчивых к ярлычкам (shortcuts), при обучении глубоких агентов поиска. Авторы формализуют проблему того, что сложные графовые структуры не гарантируют реальную сложность поиска, так как модель может найти более дешёвый путь к ответу вместо выполнения предполагаемого поиска. Они выявляют четыре типа рисков ярлычков и предлагают методы их контроля на этапах выбора сущностей, построения графов доказательств, формулирования вопросов и состязательной доработки. Эксперименты показывают, что FORT создаёт датасеты, где агентам требуется более глубокий поиск, и обученный на них поисковик достигает лучших результатов на сложных бенчмарках.

en.title: Building Smarter Search Agents with Shortcut-Resistant Data
en.desc: This paper presents a new framework called FORT, designed to create training data for deep search agents that avoids common shortcuts that can lead to misleading results. It identifies four specific risks that can cause shortcuts in data synthesis, such as relying on single clues or prior knowledge. By analyzing search trajectories, the framework helps ensure that the training data promotes genuine search difficulty rather than exploiting easier paths. The experiments demonstrate that FORT leads to improved search performance and longer search times, making it a valuable tool for developing more effective search agents.

zh.title: 构建抗捷径训练数据的创新框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种创建抗捷径训练数据的框架，旨在为深度搜索代理识别和减轻四种捷径风险。通过引入捷径意识的难度框架，作者识别了证据共覆盖、单线索选择性、暴露常量和先验知识绑定等四种可操作的捷径风险。该框架通过控制实体选择、证据图构建、问题制定和对抗性优化等过程，构建抗捷径的训练数据。实验结果表明，使用该框架生成的数据在搜索过程中表现出更长的前答案搜索时间和更少的捷径模式。

papers.title: LabVLA: Grounding Vision-Language-Action Models in Scientific Laboratories
papers.abstract: Abstract LabVLA, a vision-language-action model trained with a two-stage approach combining action token pretraining and flow matching, demonstrates superior performance on laboratory automation tasks through simulated data generation and robot-specific learning.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Scientific laboratories increasingly rely on AI systems to reason about experiments, but the physical act of doing science remains largely outside their reach. AI can help read literature, generate hypotheses, and plan protocols, yet the execution of those protocols at the bench still requires a human operator. Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models provide one possible interface between written protocols and robot execution, but existing policies are trained mostly on household and tabletop demonstrations and rarely encounter the instruments, transparent liquids, or fixed protocol workflows found in scientific laboratories. Closing this gap requires both laboratory-specific supervision and a unified learning framework that can accommodate the diverse robot embodiments used to execute experimental protocols. We therefore identify data and embodiment as central bottlenecks alongside model design. To address the data side, we build RoboGenesis, a simulation-based workflow and data engine that composes configured laboratory workflows from atomic skills, validates and filters rollouts, and exports structured demonstrations across supported robot profiles. On the policy side, we present LabVLA, trained with a two-stage recipe: FAST action token pretraining first makes the Qwen3-VL-4B-Instruct backbone action aware before any continuous control is learned, and flow matching posttraining then attaches a DiT action expert under knowledge insulation. On the LabUtopia benchmark, LabVLA achieves the highest average success rate among all evaluated baselines under both in-distribution and out-of-distribution settings.

ru.title: Робототехника для науки: обучение мультимодальных моделей на синтетических лабораторных данных
ru.desc: LabVLA — это модель vision-language-action, специально разработанная для автоматизации лабораторных работ. Модель обучается двухэтапным методом: сначала происходит предварительное обучение токенов действий (action token pretraining), затем применяется flow matching для подстройки диффузионной модели. Для решения проблемы нехватки лабораторных данных авторы создали RoboGenesis — симуляционный конвейер, который генерирует синтетические демонстрации из атомарных навыков и адаптирует их под разные робототехнические платформы. LabVLA демонстрирует лучшую производительность на бенчмарке LabUtopia как при распределении внутри обучающей выборки, так и при выходе за её пределы.

en.title: Bridging Science and Robotics with LabVLA
en.desc: LabVLA is a vision-language-action model designed to improve laboratory automation by integrating action token pretraining and flow matching techniques. It addresses the challenge of executing scientific protocols by bridging the gap between written instructions and robotic execution, which has been limited by existing models trained on non-laboratory tasks. The model utilizes a simulation-based data engine called RoboGenesis to generate and validate structured demonstrations tailored for various robotic systems. As a result, LabVLA outperforms other models on the LabUtopia benchmark, showcasing its effectiveness in both familiar and novel laboratory environments.

zh.title: LabVLA：连接实验室与机器人执行的桥梁
zh.desc: LabVLA是一种视觉-语言-动作模型，通过结合动作标记预训练和流匹配的两阶段方法，展示了在实验室自动化任务中的优越性能。该模型利用模拟数据生成和特定于机器人的学习，解决了科学实验中AI与实际操作之间的差距。为此，研究者们构建了RoboGenesis，一个基于模拟的工作流和数据引擎，能够生成和验证实验室工作流程。LabVLA在LabUtopia基准测试中，在所有评估的基线中实现了最高的平均成功率，显示了其在科学实验中的应用潜力。

papers.title: HYDRA-X: Native Unified Multimodal Models with Holistic Visual Tokenizers
papers.abstract: Abstract HYDRA-X presents a unified multimodal model that integrates image and video tokenization within a single Vision Transformer, addressing spatiotemporal reconstruction and semantic awareness through causal temporal attention and hierarchical compression.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Holistic visual tokenizers are fundamental to unified multimodal models (UMMs) as they map diverse visual inputs into a unified representation space. In this paper, we present HYDRA-X, the first UMM that unifies image and video tokenization within a single Vision Transformer (ViT). Our design is driven by two core challenges: efficiently injecting spatiotemporal reconstruction capability into a native ViT, and embedding image- and video-level semantic awareness into the latent space. To address the first, comprehensive ablations reveal two key findings: (1) frame-level causal temporal attention suffices for visual reconstruction, whereas full spatiotemporal attention degrades it; and (2) hierarchical temporal compression substantially outperforms single-step alternatives. To tackle the second, we propose a lightweight decompressor that upsamples temporally compressed features under joint image-video teacher supervision, thereby enforcing complementary semantic structures within the compact latent space. Building on this holistic tokenizer, we further propose a principled improvement of the editing pipeline: source-target interaction should occur at the latent level inside the tokenizer rather than at the semantic level inside the LLM, substantially improving editing consistency and accelerating convergence. Instantiated at the 7B dense model, HYDRA-X achieves strong performance across image and video understanding and generation tasks, paving the way for future unified-tokenizer UMMs.

ru.title: Единый токенизатор для изображений и видео через Vision Transformer
ru.desc: HYDRA-X представляет единый мультимодальный вейв на основе Vision Transformer, который объединяет токенизацию изображений и видео в одной архитектуре. Для решения задачи пространственно-временной реконструкции модель использует причинное временное внимание на уровне кадров и иерархическую компрессию временных признаков. Авторы предлагают лёгкий декодер, который под супервизией учителя на изображениях и видео заставляет скрытое пространство учиться дополнительным семантическим структурам. Модель показывает сильные результаты на задачах понимания и генерации изображений и видео, устанавливая новый стандарт для единых мультимодальных моделей.

en.title: Unifying Image and Video Understanding with HYDRA-X
en.desc: HYDRA-X introduces a unified multimodal model that combines image and video tokenization using a single Vision Transformer (ViT). It addresses the challenges of spatiotemporal reconstruction and semantic awareness by employing causal temporal attention and hierarchical compression techniques. The model demonstrates that frame-level causal attention is effective for visual reconstruction, while hierarchical temporal compression enhances performance compared to simpler methods. Additionally, it improves the editing process by allowing interactions at the latent level, leading to better consistency and faster results in image and video tasks.

zh.title: HYDRA-X：统一图像与视频的多模态模型
zh.desc: HYDRA-X 是一个统一的多模态模型，结合了图像和视频的标记化，使用单一的视觉变换器（ViT）。该模型通过因果时间注意力和分层压缩来解决时空重建和语义感知的问题。研究表明，帧级因果时间注意力足以进行视觉重建，而全时空注意力会导致性能下降。此外，HYDRA-X 还提出了一种轻量级解压器，能够在图像和视频的教师监督下提升时间压缩特征，从而增强紧凑潜在空间中的语义结构。

papers.title: N-GRPO: Embedding-Level Neighbor Mixing for Enhanced Policy Optimization
papers.abstract: Abstract N-GRPO, a novel exploration strategy within GRPO framework, enhances mathematical reasoning in large language models through semantic neighbor mixing that maintains semantic consistency while injecting diversity.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct The success of Large Language Models in mathematical reasoning relies heavily on the generation of diverse and valid solution paths during the rollout phase. However, current rollout techniques face a fundamental trade-off: token-level sampling often yields redundant trajectories that differ only in rephrasing, while embedding-level methods utilizing random noise frequently disrupt semantic consistency. To resolve this, we introduce N-GRPO, a novel exploration strategy integrated into the Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) framework. Rather than relying on token-level sampling or native embedding-level noise, our approach leverages Semantic Neighbor Mixing. This mechanism dynamically constructs input representations by mixing the embeddings of an anchor token and its nearest semantic neighbors, thereby injecting diversity while strictly adhering to the local semantic manifold. Experimental evaluations on the DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen models across different sizes show that N-GRPO not only achieves consistent improvements over strong baselines on math reasoning benchmarks but also exhibits robust generalization capabilities on out-of-distribution tasks.

ru.title: Разнообразие через семантическое соседство: N-GRPO для математического рассуждения
ru.desc: В этой работе предложен N-GRPO — новый метод исследования пространства решений для улучшения математического рассуждения в больших языковых моделях. Авторы решают проблему баланса между разнообразием генерируемых путей решения и сохранением семантической согласованности, используя технику семантического микширования соседних эмбеддингов. Метод интегрирован в фреймворк Group Relative Policy Optimization и динамически конструирует входные представления путём смешивания эмбеддингов якорного токена с его ближайшими семантическими соседями. Экспериментальные результаты на моделях DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen демонстрируют улучшение производительности на бенчмарках математического рассуждения и хорошую обобщаемость на out-of-distribution задачах.

en.title: Enhancing Math Reasoning with Semantic Neighbor Mixing
en.desc: The paper introduces N-GRPO, a new exploration strategy designed to improve mathematical reasoning in large language models. It operates within the Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) framework and uses a technique called Semantic Neighbor Mixing to enhance diversity in solution paths while preserving semantic consistency. This approach avoids the pitfalls of traditional token-level sampling and embedding-level noise, which can lead to redundancy or loss of meaning. Experimental results demonstrate that N-GRPO significantly outperforms existing methods on math reasoning tasks and shows strong generalization on unseen data.

zh.title: N-GRPO：提升数学推理的多样性与一致性
zh.desc: N-GRPO是一种新颖的探索策略，旨在提升大型语言模型在数学推理方面的表现。它通过语义邻居混合的方法，在保持语义一致性的同时引入多样性，从而生成有效的解决路径。与传统的基于token的采样和嵌入级别的随机噪声方法相比，N-GRPO能够更好地构建输入表示，确保语义的连贯性。实验结果表明，N-GRPO在数学推理基准测试中表现优异，并在不同任务上展现出强大的泛化能力。

papers.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
papers.abstract: Abstract Environment engineering enhances autonomous scientific discovery by designing structured agent environments that optimize behaviors like exploration and collaboration while mitigating issues such as reward hacking and human oversight friction, as demonstrated by the EurekAgent system that achieves state-of-the-art results across multiple domains with low computational costs.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct LLM-based agents have shown increasing potential in automating scientific discovery. Given an optimizable metric and an execution environment, they can propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions, and have produced results that outperform human-designed approaches. As model capabilities continue to improve, we argue that the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from prescribing agent workflows to designing agent environments: the resources, constraints, and interfaces that shape agent behavior. We frame this as environment engineering: building environments that amplify productive behaviors, such as open-ended exploration, systematic artifact management, and inter-agent collaboration, while suppressing harmful behaviors, such as reward hacking and high-friction human oversight. We present EurekAgent, an environment-engineered agent system for metric-driven autonomous scientific discovery. EurekAgent engineers the environment along four dimensions: permissions engineering for bounded agent execution and isolated evaluation; artifact engineering for filesystem and Git-based collaboration; budget engineering for budget-aware exploration; and human-in-the-loop engineering for easy human supervision and intervention. EurekAgent sets new state-of-the-art results on multiple mathematics, kernel engineering, and machine learning tasks, including new state-of-the-art 26-circle packing results discovered with less than $11 in total API cost. We open-source our code and results, and call for environment engineering as a core research direction for developing reliable autonomous research agents.
affiliations: Department of Computer Science and Technology, Tsinghua University

ru.title: Инженерия окружения: путь к надежным автономным исследовательским агентам
ru.desc: В статье представляется концепция инженерии окружения для повышения эффективности автономных агентов в научных исследованиях. Авторы утверждают, что узким местом является не проектирование рабочих процессов агентов, а конструирование их окружения с учётом ресурсов, ограничений и интерфейсов. Система EurekAgent демонстрирует, как целенаправленное проектирование окружения по четырём измерениям — инженерия разрешений, управление артефактами, бюджет исследования и взаимодействие с человеком — усиливает продуктивное поведение и подавляет вредоносное. Система достигает новых рекордов на задачах из математики, машинного обучения и оптимизации при минимальных вычислительных затратах.

en.title: Empowering Autonomous Discovery through Environment Engineering
en.desc: This paper discusses the concept of environment engineering, which focuses on creating structured environments for autonomous agents to enhance scientific discovery. The EurekAgent system exemplifies this approach by optimizing agent behaviors like exploration and collaboration while minimizing issues such as reward hacking. The authors argue that as machine learning models improve, the challenge lies in designing effective environments rather than just workflows for agents. EurekAgent demonstrates significant advancements in various scientific tasks, achieving state-of-the-art results with low computational costs and emphasizing the importance of environment engineering in future research.

zh.title: 环境工程：自主科学发现的新方向
zh.desc: 环境工程通过设计结构化的代理环境来增强自主科学发现，优化探索和协作等行为，同时减少奖励黑客和人类监督摩擦等问题。EurekAgent系统在多个领域中以低计算成本实现了最先进的结果。随着模型能力的不断提高，自主科学发现的瓶颈正在从规定代理工作流程转向设计代理环境。我们提出环境工程作为核心研究方向，以构建能够促进开放式探索和系统化管理的环境，同时抑制有害行为。

papers.title: Demystifying Hidden-State Recurrence: Switchable Latent Reasoning with On-Policy Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract A switchable latent reasoning framework uses explicit boundary tokens to enable trainable and interpretable latent reasoning through recurrent hidden states.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Latent chain-of-thought compresses reasoning by replacing visible reasoning traces with continuous hidden-state recurrence, but existing formulations are difficult to optimize with standard on-policy reinforcement learning (RL) and hard to interpret causally. Our key insight is that a single pair of explicit boundary tokens can address both issues at once: discrete entry and exit anchors make the latent block compatible with standard on-policy RL, and the same anchors offer a natural foothold for mechanistic analysis. Motivated by this, we propose SWITCH, a switchable latent reasoning framework. The model emits <swi> to enter latent mode and </swi> to exit. Because the boundaries are ordinary discrete tokens, the GRPO policy ratio is well-defined at every decision point. The same anchors also expose the latent steps to direct probing and causal intervention. We train the model with a visible-to-latent curriculum and a Switch-GRPO objective that propagates gradients through recurrent latent computation. SWITCH consistently outperforms prior hidden-state-recurrence latent reasoning approaches at similar scale. Mechanistic analysis through the boundary tokens further reveals three findings: (i) <swi> is a sharply localised, learned switching policy rather than a stylistic artefact; (ii) the latent step it opens performs problem-specific, causally important computation rather than acting as an inert placeholder; and (iii) that computation is concentrated at a single hidden-state transition on entry. Together, these results show that hidden-state-recurrence latent reasoning is both RL-trainable and open to direct mechanistic analysis, including of how on-policy RL itself improves the model from the inside.

ru.title: Явные границы скрытого мышления: интерпретируемое и оптимизируемое латентное рассуждение
ru.desc: В работе предложена SWITCH — структурированная система для скрытого рассуждения, использующая явные граничные токены для входа и выхода из режима латентного вычисления. Модель генерирует специальные токены <swi> и </swi>, которые делают скрытые вычисления совместимыми со стандартным обучением с подкреплением на текущей политике (on-policy RL). Авторы применили curriculum learning для постепенного перехода от явного рассуждения к скрытому и разработали objetivo Switch-GRPO для оптимизации через рекуррентные латентные вычисления. Механистический анализ показывает, что граничные токены выполняют критически важные вычисления, специфичные для задачи, что делает скрытое рассуждение одновременно обучаемым и интерпретируемым.

en.title: SWITCH: Enhancing Latent Reasoning with Boundary Tokens
en.desc: The paper introduces SWITCH, a novel framework for latent reasoning that utilizes explicit boundary tokens to enhance trainability and interpretability. By employing discrete tokens to mark entry and exit points in the reasoning process, the model aligns well with standard on-policy reinforcement learning (RL) techniques. This approach not only simplifies optimization but also allows for causal analysis of the reasoning steps. The findings demonstrate that the framework effectively combines latent reasoning with RL training, revealing important insights into the model's internal computations.

zh.title: 可切换的潜在推理框架
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种可切换的潜在推理框架，使用显式边界标记来实现可训练和可解释的潜在推理。通过引入边界标记，模型能够与标准的在线强化学习兼容，并且便于进行机制分析。研究表明，这种框架在推理过程中能够有效地压缩推理链，并且在训练过程中表现优于以往的潜在推理方法。最终，论文展示了如何通过边界标记进行直接的因果干预和分析，从而提升模型的性能。

papers.title: VideoMDM: Towards 3D Human Motion Generation From 2D Supervision
papers.abstract: Abstract VideoMDM trains 3D human motion priors from 2D poses using a diffusion framework with 2D reprojection loss and 3D motion regularizers, achieving near-3D supervised performance without requiring 3D ground truth.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We introduce VideoMDM, a diffusion-based framework that trains 3D human motion priors directly from accurate 2D poses extracted from monocular videos, without any 3D ground truth. A pretrained 2D-to-3D lifter provides approximate 3D pose sequences that serve as a noisy teacher: these are diffused, denoised by the model in 3D, and supervised in 2D by reprojecting the prediction and comparing against accurate keypoints. We show that, under mild assumptions, a depth-weighted 2D reprojection loss is equivalent in expectation to direct 3D supervision, and we adapt standard 3D motion regularizers - velocity consistency and over-parameterized representation alignment - to this 2D setting. Unlike methods that lift 2D to 3D only at inference, VideoMDM learns a coherent 3D motion manifold during training. On HumanML3D it nearly closes the gap to fully 3D-supervised MDM (FID 0.88 vs 0.54); On real video datasets Fit3D and NBA the method learns to generate motions consistently preferred by humans, with strong quantitative results.

ru.title: Обучение 3D движений человека без 3D разметки через диффузию в 2D пространстве
ru.desc: VideoMDM — это фреймворк на основе диффузионных моделей, который обучает приоры 3D движений человека, используя только 2D позы из монокулярных видео без 3D разметки. Ключевая идея заключается в использовании предобученного 2D-to-3D лифтера в качестве зашумленного учителя и применении переопроектирования в 2D пространство для супервизии, что математически эквивалентно прямой 3D супервизии при соответствующих условиях. Метод адаптирует стандартные регуляризаторы 3D движений, такие как консистентность скорости и выравнивание переопределённых представлений, к 2D постановке. На бенчмарках HumanML3D, Fit3D и NBA модель демонстрирует результаты, близкие к полностью 3D-супервизируемым подходам, при этом генерируя естественные движения, которые люди предпочитают.

en.title: Transforming 2D Poses into Realistic 3D Motion
en.desc: VideoMDM is a novel framework that trains 3D human motion models using only 2D poses from videos, eliminating the need for 3D ground truth data. It employs a diffusion process where a pretrained 2D-to-3D lifter provides approximate 3D poses, which are then refined through denoising and 2D reprojection loss. The method incorporates 3D motion regularizers to ensure the generated motions are coherent and realistic. Results show that VideoMDM achieves performance close to fully 3D-supervised methods while producing human-preferred motion outputs.

zh.title: VideoMDM：从2D姿态学习3D运动的创新方法
zh.desc: VideoMDM是一种基于扩散框架的方法，它从单目视频中提取的2D姿态直接训练3D人类运动先验，而无需3D真实数据。该方法使用预训练的2D到3D转换器生成近似的3D姿态序列，并通过扩散和去噪过程来优化3D运动。通过将预测结果重新投影到2D并与准确的关键点进行比较，VideoMDM实现了有效的监督学习。实验结果表明，该方法在多个数据集上表现出色，接近完全3D监督的性能。

papers.title: VIA-SD: Verification via Intra-Model Routing for Speculative Decoding
papers.abstract: Abstract VIA-SD introduces a multi-tier speculative decoding framework that uses intra-model routing to reduce verification costs by employing slim submodels for medium-confidence token validation, achieving significant speedups over traditional approaches.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Speculative decoding (SD) addresses the high inference costs of LLMs by having lightweight drafters generate candidates for large verifiers to validate in parallel. Existing draft-verify methods use binary decisions: accept or fully recompute. Yet we find that many rejected tokens can be verified correctly by a slim submodel derived from the full verifier via intra-model routing, instead of the full verifier. This motivates our slim-verifier to handle tokens requiring moderate verification resources, reducing expensive large-model calls. We propose Verification via Intra-Model Routing for Speculative Decoding (VIA-SD), a multi-tier framework using a routed slim-verifier. Draft tokens are processed hierarchically: direct acceptance for high-confidence cases, slim-verifier regeneration for medium-confidence cases, and full-model verification for uncertain cases. Across four representative tasks and multiple model families, VIA-SD reduces rejection rates by 0.10-0.22 and delivers 10-20% speedups over strong SD baselines, while achieving 2.5-3x acceleration over non-drafting decoding. Moreover, VIA-SD is compatible with existing SD frameworks without modifying their training procedures. Our results suggest multi-tier SD as a general paradigm for scalable and efficient LLM inference. Project page: https://zju-xyc.github.io/VIA-SD-Project-Page/
affiliations: CFAR, Agency for Science, Technology and Research, Singapore
College of Artificial Intelligence, Zhejiang University
IHPC, Agency for Science, Technology and Research
ReLER, The State Key Lab of Brain Machine Intelligence, Zhejiang University

ru.title: Трёхуровневая верификация для ускорения инференса больших языковых моделей
ru.desc: VIA-SD предлагает многоуровневую систему спекулятивного декодирования, которая снижает вычислительные затраты при инференсе больших языковых моделей. Вместо бинарного выбора между полным принятием или полной переработкой токена, система использует маршрутизацию внутри модели для создания облегченной подмодели, которая проверяет токены средней уверенности. Фреймворк обрабатывает токены иерархически: высокоуверенные токены принимаются напрямую, среднеуверенные проходят через облегченную верификатор, а неуверенные требуют полной модели. Метод достигает ускорения на 10-20% над базовыми подходами спекулятивного декодирования и совместим с существующими SD фреймворками без переобучения.

en.title: Efficient Inference with Multi-Tier Speculative Decoding
en.desc: The paper presents VIA-SD, a multi-tier speculative decoding framework designed to enhance the efficiency of large language models (LLMs) by reducing verification costs. It introduces intra-model routing to utilize slim submodels for validating medium-confidence tokens, which allows for faster processing compared to traditional binary draft-verify methods. By categorizing tokens based on confidence levels, VIA-SD optimizes the verification process, leading to significant speed improvements and lower rejection rates. The framework is compatible with existing speculative decoding systems, making it a versatile solution for scalable LLM inference.

zh.title: 多层次推测解码：高效的LLM推理新范式
zh.desc: VIA-SD提出了一种多层次的推测解码框架，通过模型内部路由来降低验证成本，使用精简子模型进行中等置信度的标记验证，从而显著加快速度。推测解码（SD）通过轻量级草拟者生成候选项，供大型验证器并行验证，解决了大型语言模型（LLM）高推理成本的问题。现有的草拟-验证方法使用二元决策：接受或完全重新计算，而我们发现许多被拒绝的标记可以通过精简子模型进行正确验证。VIA-SD通过多层次框架和路由精简验证器，优化了标记处理流程，提高了效率并降低了拒绝率。

papers.title: Where, What, Why, and Importance: Structured Defect Grounding for Text-to-Image Feedback
papers.abstract: Abstract Structured Defect Grounding (SDG) addresses limitations in text-to-image model diagnosis by modeling defects as structured sets and using vision-language models for detection and reward-based alignment.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Despite generating increasingly photorealistic images, text-to-image (T2I) models still exhibit localized, subtle, and structurally complex failures. Diagnosing these failures requires instance-level feedback that answers where a defect occurs, what type it is, why it is defective, and its importance to overall image quality. While recent dense-feedback methods move beyond scalar supervision, their heatmap-centric representations still formulate diagnosis as pixel-field regression, making it difficult to localize variable-cardinality defects and bind semantic reasons to individual failures. To address this representation bottleneck, we propose Structured Defect Grounding (SDG), which casts T2I diagnosis as structured set prediction by modeling each defect as a (location, type, reason, importance) tuple. To make this formulation trainable and measurable, we introduce SDG-30K, a 30K-image dataset with box-grounded annotations across four modern T2I generators, together with a dedicated evaluation protocol, SDG-Eval. Building on this structured representation, we further present a diagnosis-to-alignment framework in which a Vision-Language Model (VLM) serves as the SDG detector, and BoxFlow-GRPO converts predicted defect sets into box-derived, importance-weighted spatial rewards for diffusion model alignment. Extensive experiments show that our SDG detector outperforms leading proprietary VLMs on structured defect grounding, while SDG-guided rewards consistently improve T2I alignment and support localized image refinement. These results establish SDG as a unified, instance-level interface for diagnosing, evaluating, and enhancing modern generative models.

ru.title: Структурированная диагностика дефектов в генеративных моделях через предсказание упорядоченных наборов
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод Structured Defect Grounding (SDG) для диагностики дефектов в text-to-image моделях путем моделирования ошибок как структурированных наборов с атрибутами местоположения, типа, причины и важности. Авторы создали датасет SDG-30K с аннотациями 30 тысяч изображений и разработали специальный протокол оценки SDG-Eval для измерения качества диагностики. На основе видение-языковых моделей реализован детектор дефектов, а метод BoxFlow-GRPO преобразует предсказанные наборы дефектов в пространственные награды для обучения диффузионных моделей. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что SDG-направленное обучение с подкреплением значительно улучшает качество генерируемых изображений и позволяет проводить локализованное уточнение.

en.title: Structured Defect Grounding: Enhancing T2I Model Diagnosis and Alignment
en.desc: The paper introduces Structured Defect Grounding (SDG), a new approach for diagnosing failures in text-to-image (T2I) models by representing defects as structured sets. Each defect is modeled as a tuple containing its location, type, reason, and importance, allowing for more precise identification and understanding of issues. The authors present a new dataset, SDG-30K, which includes 30,000 images with detailed annotations to facilitate training and evaluation of the SDG framework. By leveraging a Vision-Language Model for defect detection and a novel reward system for model alignment, the SDG approach significantly enhances the performance of T2I models in generating high-quality images.

zh.title: 结构化缺陷定位：提升生成模型的诊断与对齐
zh.desc: 结构缺陷定位（SDG）解决了文本到图像模型诊断中的局限性，通过将缺陷建模为结构化集合，并使用视觉-语言模型进行检测和基于奖励的对齐。尽管文本到图像模型生成的图像越来越逼真，但仍然存在局部、微妙和结构复杂的失败。SDG将诊断视为结构化集合预测，将每个缺陷建模为（位置、类型、原因、重要性）元组。通过引入SDG-30K数据集和专门的评估协议，SDG为现代生成模型的诊断、评估和增强提供了统一的实例级接口。

papers.title: From 2D Grids to 1D Tokens: Reforming Shared Representations for Multimodal Image Fusion
papers.abstract: Abstract A multimodal image fusion approach uses a 1D token interface from a pretrained image tokenizer to enhance global appearance coherence while preserving local details through selective token editing.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Multimodal image fusion aims to integrate complementary information from different modalities into a fused image that preserves rich local details while maintaining globally consistent appearance. Existing approaches build shared representations on 2D feature grids, which excel at modeling local structures but offer limited leverage over image-level global appearance factors. To balance these objectives, we introduce a compact 1D token interface based on a frozen pretrained image tokenizer for modeling non-local appearance/base factors. Rather than using the tokenizer as a reconstruction backbone, our design uses the 1D token space as a global carrier while retaining the 2D spatial pathway for local structure restoration. Specifically, we introduce Selective Token Editing (STE), which sparsely updates/replaces a small set of critical tokens, providing a lightweight mechanism to steer global appearance coherence while keeping the fusion backbone unchanged and avoiding extra losses. Experiments on four commonly used benchmarks show that our method achieves the best overall performance, with consistent, multi-metric improvements in both global coherence and local fidelity. Project page: https://zju-xyc.github.io/1D-Fusion-Project-Page/
affiliations: CFAR, Agency for Science, Technology and Research, Singapore
College of Artificial Intelligence, Zhejiang University
IHPC, Agency for Science, Technology and Research, Singapore
ReLER, The State Key Lab of Brain Machine Intelligence, Zhejiang University

ru.title: Глобальный контроль через редкое редактирование токенов при слиянии изображений
ru.desc: В статье предложен новый подход к многомодальному слиянию изображений, который использует одномерный интерфейс токенов от предобученного токенизатора для балансировки глобальной согласованности и локальных деталей. Авторы вводят механизм выборочного редактирования токенов (Selective Token Editing), который разреженно обновляет критические токены для управления глобальной когерентностью образа без изменения основной архитектуры. Метод сохраняет двумерный пространственный канал для восстановления локальной структуры, используя одномерное пространство токенов как глобальный носитель информации. Экспериментальная оценка на четырех стандартных наборах данных показывает превосходство предложенного подхода в улучшении как глобальной согласованности, так и локальной точности.

en.title: Fusing Images with Global Coherence and Local Detail
en.desc: This paper presents a novel multimodal image fusion technique that utilizes a 1D token interface derived from a pretrained image tokenizer. The approach aims to enhance the overall appearance of fused images while preserving intricate local details through a method called Selective Token Editing (STE). By using a compact 1D token space for global appearance factors and maintaining a 2D spatial pathway for local structures, the method effectively balances global coherence and local fidelity. Experimental results demonstrate that this technique outperforms existing methods across multiple benchmarks, achieving significant improvements in both aspects.

zh.title: 多模态图像融合：全局一致性与局部细节的平衡
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种多模态图像融合方法，利用预训练图像标记器的1D令牌接口来增强全局外观一致性，同时保留局部细节。该方法通过选择性令牌编辑（STE）来更新关键令牌，从而在保持融合基础结构不变的情况下，轻松调整全局外观。与传统的2D特征网格方法相比，这种方法在建模非局部外观因素方面表现更佳。实验结果表明，该方法在多个基准测试中实现了最佳性能，展现了全局一致性和局部保真度的显著提升。

papers.title: MoVerse: Real-Time Video World Modeling with Panoramic Gaussian Scaffold
papers.abstract: Abstract MoVerse generates real-time interactive video from single images by creating 360° panoramas and 3D Gaussian scaffolds, enabling efficient rendering through diffusion-based techniques.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We present MoVerse, a real-time video world model that creates an interactively navigable scene from a single narrow-field-of-view image. This setting is challenging because the input observes only a small fraction of the environment, while interactive roaming requires a complete surrounding world, persistent geometry, controllable camera motion, and temporally coherent high-fidelity observations. MoVerse addresses this problem by separating world construction from observation rendering. It first expands the input into a gravity-aligned 360^circ panorama with topology-aware diffusion, closing the missing field of view before 3D reasoning. It then lifts the panorama into a persistent 3D Gaussian scaffold using panoramic geometry-aware residual prediction, yielding a dense and directly renderable spatial memory. Finally, a Gaussian-conditioned video renderer translates scaffold renderings along user-specified camera trajectories into photorealistic video. To make this renderer practical for interaction, we train a bidirectional diffusion teacher for high-quality conditional rendering and distill it into a causal autoregressive student for bounded-latency streaming. This design combines the controllability and long-range consistency of explicit 3D representations with the perceptual quality of generative video models. MoVerse supports real-time scene roaming at 8~FPS on a single NVIDIA RTX~4090 GPU, demonstrating a practical path toward single-image world creation with interactive video output.
affiliations: Orange Team, Youku Moku-Lab, HUJING Digital Media & Entertainment Group

ru.title: От одного фото к интерактивному миру в 360°
ru.desc: MoVerse — это модель видео-мира в реальном времени, которая создаёт интерактивно навигируемую 3D сцену из одного изображения. Система расширяет входное изображение в полнопанорамный вид на 360° с помощью диффузионной модели, затем преобразует панораму в трёхмерный гауссовский скаффолд для хранения геометрической информации. Для синтеза фотореалистичного видео используется двунаправленная диффузионная модель-учитель, которая затем дистиллируется в причинную авторегрессивную модель для потоковой передачи с ограниченной задержкой. Система достигает реального времени в 8 кадров в секунду на одном GPU, позволяя пользователям интерактивно навигировать по сгенерированной 3D сцене.

en.title: Transforming Single Images into Interactive 3D Worlds
en.desc: MoVerse is a machine learning model that generates interactive video from a single image by creating a 360° panorama and a 3D Gaussian scaffold. It effectively addresses the challenge of limited input views by expanding the image into a complete surrounding world, allowing for controllable camera motion and coherent video output. The model uses advanced techniques like topology-aware diffusion and Gaussian-conditioned rendering to produce high-quality, photorealistic videos. This approach enables real-time scene navigation, achieving 8 frames per second on powerful GPUs, showcasing a significant advancement in single-image world creation.

zh.title: 单图像生成实时交互视频的创新之路
zh.desc: MoVerse是一种实时视频生成模型，它能够从单张图像创建可交互的360°全景视频。该模型通过将输入图像扩展为重力对齐的全景图，并利用拓扑感知的扩散技术来填补视野缺失，从而实现世界构建。接着，MoVerse将全景图提升为持久的3D高斯支架，利用几何感知的残差预测生成可直接渲染的空间记忆。最后，模型通过高斯条件视频渲染器将支架渲染转换为高保真视频，实现了在单张图像基础上进行实时场景漫游的可能性。

papers.title: TreeSeeker: Tree-Structured Trial, Error, and Return in Deep Search
papers.abstract: Abstract TreeSeeker is an inference-time framework that uses tree-structured search with branch-and-return control to manage exploration and exploitation in deep search tasks, improving performance through systematic trial-and-error decision making.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Deep search requires agents to answer complex questions through multi-step web search, browsing, evidence comparison, and synthesis. A central challenge is deciding how to search when several directions look plausible but only some will later lead to reliable evidence. If an agent greedily follows the current best-looking direction, it may keep extending a weak continuation. If it explores without discipline, it may waste budget on disconnected trials. We propose TreeSeeker, an inference-time framework for controlled trial-and-error in deep search. TreeSeeker organizes search as branch-and-return search over tree-structured states, where each branch is a tentative direction for a sub-goal. At each round, TreeSearch reads all sub-goal trees, identifies active goals, and uses textual UCB signals of value, uncertainty, and risk to select among exploiting a promising branch, exploring an uncertain alternative, or pruning an unproductive continuation and returning to an earlier branch point. TreeMem supports this control loop by keeping evidence, uncertainty, conflicts, progress, and failure cues attached to the branches that produced them, so trial outcomes can guide later decisions. Experiments on XBench-DeepSearch, BrowseComp, and BrowseComp-ZH show that TreeSeeker consistently outperforms strong open-source baselines, suggesting that explicit branch-and-return control complements stronger reasoning and tool execution.

ru.title: Управляемый поиск методом ветвления и возврата для сложных задач информационного поиска
ru.desc: TreeSeeker — это фреймворк для поиска вывода, который использует древовидный поиск с механизмом ветвления и возврата для управления исследованием и эксплуатацией при решении сложных задач глубокого поиска. Система организует поиск как исследование дерева состояний, где каждое ветвление представляет потенциальное направление для подцели, используя текстовые сигналы UCB (значение, неопределённость, риск) для выбора стратегии. TreeMem компонента сохраняет доказательства, неопределённость и результаты попыток, прикреплённые к ветвям, что позволяет прошлым исходам направлять будущие решения. Экспериментальные результаты на бенчмарках XBench-DeepSearch и BrowseComp показывают, что явное управление ветвлением и возвратом значительно улучшает производительность по сравнению с существующими методами.

en.title: Optimizing Deep Search with Tree-Structured Exploration
en.desc: TreeSeeker is a framework designed to enhance deep search tasks by using a tree-structured approach to manage exploration and exploitation effectively. It employs a branch-and-return strategy, allowing agents to make informed decisions about which search paths to pursue based on evidence and uncertainty. By organizing search efforts into sub-goal trees, TreeSeeker can dynamically adjust its focus, either exploiting promising leads or exploring new options while avoiding unproductive paths. Experimental results demonstrate that TreeSeeker significantly outperforms existing methods, highlighting the benefits of structured trial-and-error in complex search scenarios.

zh.title: 深度搜索的智能决策：TreeSeeker
zh.desc: TreeSeeker 是一个推理时框架，利用树状结构搜索和分支返回控制来管理深度搜索任务中的探索与利用。它通过系统的试错决策提高性能，帮助智能体在复杂问题中进行多步骤的网络搜索、浏览和证据比较。该框架在每一轮中读取所有子目标树，识别活跃目标，并使用文本 UCB 信号来选择是利用有前景的分支、探索不确定的替代方案，还是修剪无效的延续。实验结果表明，TreeSeeker 在多个基准测试中表现优于强大的开源基线，显示出明确的分支返回控制能够增强推理和工具执行的效果。

papers.title: HarnessBridge: Learnable Bidirectional Controller for LLM Agent Harness
papers.abstract: Abstract Learnable harness controller called HarnessBridge is introduced to parameterize agent-environment interfaces through bidirectional projections, achieving performance comparable to specialized harnesses with reduced computational overhead.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large language models are increasingly deployed as agents for long-horizon tasks, yet their performance is shaped not only by model capability and environment design, but also by the harness that mediates agent--environment interaction. Existing harnesses are largely manually engineered, making them difficult to scale as trajectories grow longer and interactions become more complex. In this work, we ask whether harness can be generated by a learnable plug-in module that can be trained in an end-to-end fashion. We introduce HarnessBridge, a lightweight learnable harness controller that parameterizes the agent--environment interface as a bidirectional projection. HarnessBridge learns two bidirectional projections: observation projection, which distills raw trajectories into compact, decision-relevant states, and action projection, which converts proposed actions into executable transitions or trajectory-grounded rejections. We train HarnessBridge on a harness supervision dataset via unified instruction tuning. On Terminal-Bench~2.0 and SWE-bench Verified, HarnessBridge matches or surpasses strong specialized harnesses while substantially reducing token usage and trajectory length, and generalizes from smaller generators to larger commercial models.

ru.title: Научись соединять агента с миром: обучаемый мост вместо ручного каркаса
ru.desc: В работе представлена HarnessBridge — обучаемый контроллер интерфейса между агентом и окружением, который параметризует это взаимодействие через двусторонние проекции. Модель включает проекцию наблюдений, которая сжимает сырые траектории в компактные состояния, релевантные для принятия решений, и проекцию действий, которая преобразует предложенные действия в исполняемые переходы или отклонения. HarnessBridge обучается на специализированном датасете через инструкционный tuning и достигает производительности, сравнимой со специализированными harnesses, при этом значительно снижая использование токенов и длину траекторий.

en.title: HarnessBridge: Efficient Learning for Agent-Environment Interaction
en.desc: The paper introduces HarnessBridge, a learnable controller designed to improve the interaction between agents and their environments in machine learning tasks. It utilizes bidirectional projections to streamline the agent-environment interface, allowing for more efficient processing of long trajectories. By training on a harness supervision dataset, HarnessBridge can distill observations into relevant states and convert actions into executable transitions. This approach not only matches the performance of specialized harnesses but also reduces computational costs and enhances scalability for complex tasks.

zh.title: HarnessBridge：智能体与环境的高效交互控制器
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种可学习的控制器，称为HarnessBridge，它通过双向投影来参数化智能体与环境之间的接口，从而实现与专用控制器相当的性能，同时减少计算开销。随着长时间任务的复杂性增加，现有的控制器主要是手动设计的，难以扩展。HarnessBridge作为一个轻量级的可学习控制器，能够通过端到端的训练生成控制器，学习观察投影和动作投影。实验结果表明，HarnessBridge在多个基准测试中表现优异，能够有效减少令牌使用和轨迹长度。

papers.title: Risk Under Pressure: Compute-Aware Evaluation of Adversarial Robustness in Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Compute-aware evaluation framework using FLOPs and risk-compute curves reveals non-monotonic effects of alignment training and varying attack costs across different harm categories.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Adversarial robustness evaluations of large language models (LLMs) typically report attack success rate (ASR) under fixed query budgets, implicitly treating all attacks as equally costly. In practice, the computational expense of different attack strategies can vary by orders of magnitude. Consequently, ASR at a fixed budget can obscure the true effort required to jailbreak a model, thereby making it hard to determine whether an attack's cost justifies its payoff to the attacker. We propose a compute-aware evaluation framework based on computational pressure, measured in cumulative floating-point operations (FLOPs), as a proxy for adversarial effort. We introduce risk-compute curves, which map compute budgets to attack risk, and derive two metrics that summarize the average pressure required for a given attack to succeed. Across ten models spanning three families and four different stages in language model training and alignment, evaluated with three attack strategies (gradient-based, iterative refinement, and template-based) on two jailbreak robustness benchmarks, we find: (1) alignment training has non-monotonic effects on compute-space robustness; (2) scaling model size reduces gradient-based attack effectiveness but has limited impact on cheaper template-based attacks; (3) gradient-based attacks optimized on a surrogate model can transfer to a separate target model, providing a way to reduce attacker costs; (4) compute cost varies by up to {approx}5{times} across harm categories within a single model; and (5) safety-aligned RL increases aggregate cost while leaving some categories disproportionately accessible. We release our framework to enable compute-aware risk assessment and evaluation.

ru.title: Вычислительно-осознанная оценка адверсариальной робастности языковых моделей
ru.desc: Исследование предлагает фреймворк для оценки устойчивости больших языковых моделей к адверсариальным атакам, основанный на вычислительных затратах (FLOPs) вместо фиксированного количества запросов. Авторы вводят кривые «риск-вычисления», которые отображают связь между бюджетом вычислений и успехом атаки, и обнаруживают немонотонные эффекты обучения выравниванию модели на её робастность. Результаты показывают, что стоимость атак существенно варьируется в зависимости от типа вреда и стратегии атаки, при этом увеличение размера модели по-разному влияет на разные типы атак. Фреймворк позволяет более точно оценивать реальные усилия, необходимые для взлома модели, и выявляет пробелы в защите даже в моделях с обучением безопасности.

en.title: Understanding Adversarial Attacks Through Compute Awareness
en.desc: This paper introduces a compute-aware evaluation framework that uses floating-point operations (FLOPs) to assess the effort required for adversarial attacks on large language models (LLMs). It highlights that traditional evaluations often overlook the varying computational costs of different attack strategies, which can misrepresent the true difficulty of breaching a model's defenses. The authors present risk-compute curves to illustrate the relationship between compute budgets and attack risks, revealing that alignment training can have complex effects on model robustness. Their findings indicate that while larger models may resist certain attacks, cheaper attack methods can still be effective, emphasizing the need for a nuanced understanding of adversarial risks.

zh.title: 计算感知评估：揭示攻击成本与鲁棒性之间的关系
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种计算感知的评估框架，利用浮点运算次数（FLOPs）和风险-计算曲线来揭示对齐训练和不同攻击成本在不同伤害类别中的非单调效应。我们发现，固定查询预算下的攻击成功率（ASR）可能掩盖了真正的攻击成本，使得评估攻击的有效性变得困难。通过对十个模型进行评估，我们发现对齐训练对计算空间的鲁棒性有非单调影响，且模型规模的扩大对基于梯度的攻击效果有限。最后，我们的框架可以帮助进行计算感知的风险评估和评估。

papers.title: High-Fidelity Two-Step Image Generation via Teacher-Aligned End-to-End Distillation
papers.abstract: Abstract A 2-step image generation model is developed through distillation from an 8-step teacher using distribution-aligned adversarial learning, step-decoupled parameterization, and end-to-end training with iterative regularization.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Few-step diffusion distillation has become increasingly mature for 4-8-step generation, yet pushing further to 2 steps remains challenging. In this work, we introduce Z-Image Turbo++, a high-quality 2-step image generation model distilled from the 8-step Z-Image Turbo teacher. Our method addresses the central bottlenecks of increased task difficulty and limited model capacity in 2-step generation through three simple but effective design choices tailored to this regime. First, we propose Distribution-Aligned Adversarial Learning, which uses teacher-generated images rather than external real images as real samples for GAN training, providing a more attainable and informative adversarial target. Second, we adopt Step-Decoupled Parameterization, assigning independent model parameters to the two denoising steps to better match their distinct capacity demands. Third, we perform End-to-End Training with Iterative Regularization, allowing the first step to receive gradients from final image quality while preserving a meaningful intermediate generation through an explicit step-1 loss. Together, these designs substantially narrow the quality gap between 2-step and 8-step generation in both qualitative and quantitative evaluations, highlighting the potential of carefully tailored distillation strategies for improving the quality-efficiency trade-off in few-step generation.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Молниеносная генерация: дистилляция диффузионной модели в 2 шага
ru.desc: В работе представлен Z-Image Turbo++, модель для генерации изображений всего за 2 шага, полученная путём дистилляции из 8-шагового учителя.
