Основной метод использует три ключевые техники: распределение-выравненное состязательное обучение с использованием изображений учителя в качестве реальных примеров, параметризацию с независимыми весами для каждого шага денойзинга, и сквозное обучение с итеративной регуляризацией для сохранения качества промежуточных результатов. Такой подход значительно сокращает разрыв в качестве между 2-шаговой и 8-шаговой генерацией, демонстрируя эффективность тщательно спроектированных стратегий дистилляции для улучшения компромисса между качеством и скоростью.

en.title: Turbocharging 2-Step Image Generation with Smart Distillation
en.desc: This paper presents Z-Image Turbo++, a novel 2-step image generation model that is distilled from an 8-step teacher model. The authors tackle the challenges of 2-step generation by introducing three key innovations: Distribution-Aligned Adversarial Learning, which uses images generated by the teacher model for training, Step-Decoupled Parameterization to optimize model capacity for each denoising step, and End-to-End Training with Iterative Regularization to enhance image quality. These strategies significantly improve the performance of 2-step generation, making it closer in quality to 8-step methods. The findings suggest that tailored distillation techniques can effectively enhance the balance between quality and efficiency in image generation tasks.

zh.title: 高效的2步图像生成模型
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种通过蒸馏技术从8步生成模型中发展而来的2步图像生成模型。我们采用了分布对齐对抗学习、步骤解耦参数化和端到端训练等方法，解决了2步生成中的任务难度和模型容量限制。特别地，使用教师生成的图像作为真实样本进行对抗训练，使得生成的图像质量更高。最终，我们的模型在定性和定量评估中显著缩小了2步与8步生成之间的质量差距，展示了蒸馏策略在少步生成中的潜力。

papers.title: Visual Para-Thinker++: A Single-Policy Multi-Agent Framework for Visual Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract A multi-agent framework with shared MLLM policy and role-specific training methods improves visual reasoning by reducing hallucinations and enabling efficient parallel processing.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Visual reasoning requires integrating evidence distributed across regions, attributes, and relations, making single-chain reasoning prone to early perceptual commitment and hallucination. We propose Visual Para-Thinker++, a single-policy multi-agent framework in which one shared MLLM policy is instantiated as role-conditioned Main, Worker, and Summary Agents. The Main Agent decomposes the task with fixed allocation patterns; Worker Agents reason in parallel under context isolation; and the Summary Agent reconciles full Worker reasoning traces rather than majority-voting on final labels. The shared policy is trained by Multi-Agent Capability Injection and Role-Decoupled Multi-Agent Optimization, which assign role-specific rewards and advantages to corresponding token segments to reduce gradient conflict among collaborative roles. A native inference engine enables efficient multi-agent rollout through shared visual prefix and KV cache reuse. Across V*, CountBench, the RefCOCO family, and HallusionBench, Visual Para-Thinker++ consistently outperforms single-trajectory and inference-time parallel baselines, with especially strong gains on hallucination-sensitive visual reasoning.
affiliations: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Многоагентное рассуждение с единой политикой: преодоление галлюцинаций через ролевое разложение
ru.desc: В работе предложена система Visual Para-Thinker++, которая использует единую политику большой мультимодальной модели (MLLM), развёрнутую в виде нескольких агентов с разными ролями для улучшения визуального рассуждения. Основной агент декомпозирует задачу, рабочие агенты проводят параллельное рассуждение в условиях изоляции контекста, а итоговый агент примиряет полные трассы рассуждений рабочих вместо голосования по финальным ответам. Модель обучается специальными методами Multi-Agent Capability Injection и Role-Decoupled Multi-Agent Optimization, которые назначают роль-специфичные награды отдельным частям токенов для снижения конфликтов градиентов. Подход демонстрирует значительное снижение галлюцинаций и эффективную параллельную обработку на множественных бенчмарках.

en.title: Enhancing Visual Reasoning with Multi-Agent Collaboration
en.desc: This paper introduces Visual Para-Thinker++, a multi-agent framework designed to enhance visual reasoning in machine learning. It employs a shared MLLM policy with specialized roles for Main, Worker, and Summary Agents, allowing for efficient parallel processing and reducing the risk of hallucinations. The framework utilizes role-specific training methods to optimize collaboration among agents, minimizing conflicts during learning. Experimental results demonstrate that Visual Para-Thinker++ significantly outperforms traditional methods, particularly in tasks sensitive to hallucinations.

zh.title: 提升视觉推理的多智能体框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种多智能体框架，名为Visual Para-Thinker++，通过共享的多语言大模型（MLLM）策略和角色特定的训练方法来提升视觉推理能力。该框架包括主智能体、工作智能体和总结智能体，主智能体负责任务分解，工作智能体在隔离的上下文中并行推理，而总结智能体则整合工作智能体的推理结果。通过多智能体能力注入和角色解耦优化，框架有效减少了协作角色之间的梯度冲突。实验结果表明，Visual Para-Thinker++在多个基准测试中表现优于传统的单轨迹推理方法，尤其在处理幻觉敏感的视觉推理任务时效果显著。

papers.title: SG-OPD: Sign-Gated On-Policy Distillation via Sign-Consistency Gating and Phased Teacher Sampling
papers.abstract: Abstract Sign-Gated On-Policy Distillation improves upon standard on-policy distillation by incorporating a binary verifier to filter teacher signals, resulting in better performance on mathematical reasoning tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct On-policy distillation (OPD) trains a student on its own trajectories with dense per-token supervision from a stronger teacher, and often outperforms off-policy distillation and standard reinforcement learning. However, we find that its effectiveness implicitly relies on two assumptions that frequently break in practice: trajectory-level alignment between the student and the teacher, and uniform token-level reliability of the teacher's preferences. We therefore propose Sign-Gated On-Policy Distillation (SG-OPD), which uses a binary verifier as a trust signal for the teacher at two complementary granularities: phased teacher sampling mixes in verifier-endorsed teacher rollouts at cold-start, and a sign-consistency gate extrapolates the distillation update on tokens where the teacher agrees with the verifier-correct direction and interpolates it where it disagrees. Experiments on competition-level mathematical reasoning benchmarks show that SG-OPD consistently outperforms standard OPD, with average gains of 1.98 and 7.50 at the per-sample and per-question levels, respectively.

ru.title: Доверительная фильтрация учительских сигналов через бинарную верификацию
ru.desc: В статье предлагается улучшенный метод дистилляции на собственных траекториях (OPD), называемый Sign-Gated On-Policy Distillation (SG-OPD), который использует бинарный верификатор для фильтрации сигналов от учителя. Авторы выявили, что стандартная OPD полагается на два предположения, которые часто нарушаются на практике: выравнивание траекторий студента и учителя, а также надежность предпочтений учителя на уровне токенов. Предложенный метод применяет верификатор на двух уровнях детализации: фазовое смешивание одобренных учителем переходов в начале обучения и гейт согласованности знака, который экстраполирует обновления где учитель согласен с верификатором и интерполирует их в случае разногласия. Экспериментальная оценка на бенчмарках математического рассуждения показала устойчивое улучшение SG-OPD над стандартной OPD с средними прибавками 1.98 и 7.50 на уровне примеров и задач соответственно.

en.title: Enhancing Learning with Trust: Sign-Gated On-Policy Distillation
en.desc: The paper introduces Sign-Gated On-Policy Distillation (SG-OPD), which enhances traditional on-policy distillation by using a binary verifier to filter the signals from a teacher model. This method addresses the limitations of standard on-policy distillation, which relies on the assumption that the teacher's guidance is always reliable and aligned with the student's learning trajectory. By incorporating a verifier, SG-OPD ensures that only trustworthy teacher signals are used, improving the quality of the learning process. Experiments demonstrate that SG-OPD significantly outperforms standard on-policy distillation in mathematical reasoning tasks, showcasing its effectiveness in real-world applications.

zh.title: 改进的在线策略蒸馏方法提升数学推理能力
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种改进的在线策略蒸馏方法，称为Sign-Gated On-Policy Distillation（SG-OPD）。该方法通过引入一个二元验证器来过滤教师信号，从而提高在数学推理任务上的表现。SG-OPD在冷启动阶段混合了经过验证器认可的教师样本，并在教师与验证器一致的情况下进行蒸馏更新。实验结果表明，SG-OPD在竞争级数学推理基准测试中，平均提升了1.98和7.50的表现。

papers.title: EvoBrowseComp: Benchmarking Search Agents on Evolving Knowledge
papers.abstract: Abstract EvoBrowseComp is an evolving benchmark with 800 contamination-free questions synthesized through a three-agent framework that ensures temporal freshness and prevents parametric memorization in search agent evaluation.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Search Agents -- large language models augmented with search tools -- have intensified the need for future-proof evaluation benchmarks. Existing benchmarks such as BrowseComp rely on static knowledge, making them vulnerable to test-set contamination and parametric memorization. Consequently, models can achieve high scores through fact recall rather than genuine retrieval, obscuring true browsing competence via reasoning shortcuts.   In this paper, we introduce EvoBrowseComp, an evolving benchmark of 400 English and 400 Chinese contamination-free complex questions synthesized via live-web traversal. To collect these questions, we design a three-agent collaborative framework: (1) a QA synthesis agent that retrieves fresh knowledge from the live web to synthesize QA pairs; (2) an information filtering agent that filters retrieved knowledge in terms of credibility and popularity to block parametric shortcuts; and (3) a high-level guidance agent that formalizes questions into reasoning graphs to reduce logical redundancy and shortcuts in synthesized QA pairs. Because the framework supports fully automated synthesis, EvoBrowseComp can be regularly updated to prevent data contamination and maintain temporal freshness. Extensive experiments confirm its great difficulty, requiring broad horizontal search. It establishes a scalable paradigm for auto-updatable, high-difficulty benchmarking that keeps pace with both evolving world knowledge and advancing agent capabilities.

ru.title: Живой бенчмарк: защита от памяти через постоянное обновление
ru.desc: В статье представлен EvoBrowseComp — динамически обновляемый бенчмарк для оценки поисковых агентов, содержащий 800 вопросов на английском и китайском языках, защищённых от утечки данных и параметрического запоминания. Бенчмарк синтезируется автоматически через трёхагентную систему: агент генерирует вопросы на основе свежей информации из живого веб-поиска, агент фильтрации проверяет достоверность источников, а направляющий агент структурирует вопросы в графы рассуждений. Благодаря полной автоматизации процесса, бенчмарк регулярно обновляется для поддержания актуальности знаний и предотвращения контаминации тестовых данных. Экспериментальная оценка показывает высокую сложность задач и требует от моделей глубокого горизонтального поиска информации.

en.title: EvoBrowseComp: A Fresh Approach to Evaluating Search Agents
en.desc: EvoBrowseComp is a new benchmark designed to evaluate search agents using 800 complex questions that are free from contamination. It employs a three-agent framework to ensure that the questions remain fresh and prevent models from simply memorizing answers. This benchmark addresses the limitations of existing static benchmarks by synthesizing questions through live web traversal, which enhances the evaluation of reasoning skills. The automated nature of EvoBrowseComp allows for regular updates, ensuring that it remains relevant as both knowledge and agent capabilities evolve.

zh.title: EvoBrowseComp：动态更新的高难度基准测试
zh.desc: EvoBrowseComp是一个不断发展的基准测试，包含800个无污染的问题，旨在评估搜索代理的能力。它通过一个三代理框架生成，确保了时间的新鲜性并防止参数记忆。与现有的静态基准测试不同，EvoBrowseComp能够定期更新，避免数据污染，保持问题的时效性。该基准测试的设计使得模型在评估时需要进行广泛的搜索，而不仅仅是简单的事实回忆。

papers.title: Rethinking Psychometric Evaluation of LLMs: When and Why Self-Reports Predict Behavior
papers.abstract: Abstract Psychometric assessments of LLM behavior reveal that specific behavioral frameworks like Theory of Planned Behavior show better coherence with actual responses than broad personality traits, particularly within shared conversations.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Anticipating LLM behavioral tendencies from low-cost psychometric probes is critical for safe deployment, but only if self-reports (SR) reliably predict behavior. Recent work documented substantial SR-behavior dissociation in LLMs, but relied on broad personality traits (Big 5) that predict specific behaviors weakly, even in humans. Furthermore, the isolation of conversational sessions combined with weak context matching left open whether LLMs truly lack coherence or whether the conditions needed to detect such coherence were not met. We contrast Big 5 with the Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB), which measures intention targeted to a specific behavior and predicts human behavior substantially better than broad traits. We run experiments across four behavioral tasks and 11 frontier LLMs, while also varying session context and identity induction. We find that SR-behavior coherence exists but is selective. 1) Within a shared conversation, the Theory of Planned Behavior reaches human-level coherence; Big 5 does not. 2) Across separate conversations, coherence survives only for behaviors anchored outside the immediate prompt, such as implicit bias shaped by training, and collapses when behavior is strongly primed by context, as with sycophancy. 3) Persona prompting makes self-reports more consistent across conversations, but does not bring behavior into alignment. These findings suggest that coarse personality frameworks, such as Big 5 may not be the best tools for testing deployment behavior. More task- and behavior-specific instruments are needed, and even these must be evaluated across tasks and contexts.

ru.title: Целевые поведенческие шкалы точнее описывают LLM, чем общие черты личности
ru.desc: Исследование показывает, что поведение больших языковых моделей лучше всего предсказывается не общими чертами личности, а специализированными психометрическими шкалами, ориентированными на конкретное поведение. Авторы сравнили классический подход Big 5 с Теорией планируемого поведения (TPB) и обнаружили, что TPB достигает согласованности на уровне человеческих показателей внутри одного разговора. Выяснилось, что самоотчеты языковых моделей согласуются с их поведением избирательно: согласованность сохраняется для поведения, независимого от контекста подсказок, но разрушается для поведений, сильно определяемых текущими инструкциями. Результаты указывают на необходимость разработки более специфичных инструментов оценки, адаптированных к конкретным задачам и контекстам, для надёжного развёртывания языковых моделей.

en.title: Unlocking LLM Behavior: The Power of Intent Over Personality
en.desc: This paper investigates how well different psychological frameworks predict the behavior of large language models (LLMs) during conversations. It finds that the Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB) is more effective than broad personality traits like the Big 5 in predicting LLM responses in shared conversations. The study shows that while self-reports can align with LLM behavior, this alignment is selective and context-dependent. The authors argue for the need for more specific assessment tools to better understand and predict LLM behavior in various contexts.

zh.title: 计划行为理论优于大五人格预测LLM行为
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了大型语言模型（LLM）行为的心理测量评估，发现计划行为理论（TPB）在预测实际反应方面比广泛的人格特质（如大五人格）更有效。研究表明，LLM的自我报告与实际行为之间存在显著的差异，尤其是在共享对话中。通过对四个行为任务和11个前沿LLM的实验，结果显示TPB在共享对话中达到了人类水平的一致性，而大五人格则未能做到。论文建议，使用更具体的任务和行为测量工具来评估LLM的行为，以确保安全部署。

papers.title: MaskAlign: Token-Subset Representation Alignment for Efficient Diffusion Training
papers.abstract: Abstract Token-subset representation alignment method called MaskAlign improves diffusion transformer training by reducing reliance on complete token sets and maintaining stable alignment behavior under perturbations.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Representation alignment with pretrained vision models has recently shown strong potential for accelerating diffusion transformer training. By aligning intermediate diffusion features with clean-image representations from self-supervised vision encoders, existing methods improve convergence and generation quality. However, such alignment also introduces a non-trivial constraint: diffusion models operate on noisy inputs whose usable information varies across timesteps, while the reference features are extracted from clean images. In this paper, we revisit this mismatch from a token-level perspective. We find that, under full-token representation alignment, tokens with large alignment-gradient norms exhibit a stable spatial preference, suggesting that the alignment objective does not affect all tokens uniformly and may encourage the model to rely on the complete set of clean-image tokens. To address this issue, we propose MaskAlign, a token-subset representation alignment method that applies alignment to randomly sampled token subsets during training. By exposing the model to different token subsets across iterations, MaskAlign reduces the dependence of representation alignment on the complete token set and encourages alignment behavior that is more stable under token-subset perturbations. To mitigate the information loss caused by directly dropping tokens, we further introduce a lightweight pre-mask token mixing block that shares information across tokens before masking.
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Выравнивание представлений через случайные подмножества токенов
ru.desc: В статье предлагается метод MaskAlign для улучшения обучения диффузионных трансформеров путём выравнивания представлений (representation alignment) с предобученными моделями зрения. Авторы обнаружили, что при полном выравнивании всех токенов модель становится чрезмерно зависимой от полного набора чистых изображений, что приводит к нестабильности при возмущениях. Предложенный метод применяет выравнивание только к случайно выбранным подмножествам токенов во время обучения, что снижает эту зависимость и делает выравнивание более устойчивым. Дополнительно вводится лёгкий блок перемешивания токенов перед маскированием для компенсации потери информации.

en.title: Enhancing Diffusion Transformers with Token-Subset Alignment
en.desc: The paper introduces MaskAlign, a method that enhances the training of diffusion transformers by focusing on subsets of tokens instead of requiring all tokens for alignment. This approach helps maintain stable alignment behavior even when some tokens are perturbed or missing. By aligning intermediate diffusion features with representations from self-supervised vision models, MaskAlign improves both the convergence speed and the quality of generated outputs. Additionally, a pre-mask token mixing block is introduced to share information among tokens, reducing the impact of information loss when tokens are dropped during training.

zh.title: MaskAlign：提升扩散变换器训练的稳定性
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为MaskAlign的子集表示对齐方法，旨在改善扩散变换器的训练。该方法通过在训练过程中随机抽取令牌子集进行对齐，减少了对完整令牌集的依赖，从而提高了对齐行为的稳定性。研究发现，完整令牌对齐会导致模型对干净图像令牌的过度依赖，而MaskAlign则通过不同的令牌子集暴露模型，缓解了这一问题。此外，本文还引入了一种轻量级的预掩码令牌混合模块，以在掩码之前共享令牌间的信息，进一步减少信息损失。

papers.title: Evoflux: Inference-Time Evolution of Executable Tool Workflows for Compact Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Evoflux enables compact language models to execute tool workflows more reliably by using evolutionary search to repair failed plans during inference, significantly improving execution feasibility compared to traditional fine-tuning methods.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Compact language models (LMs) reduce cost, latency, and deployment risk for tool agents. Yet MCP-style tool use requires more than isolated function calling: an agent must discover tools from live catalogs, satisfy schemas, preserve dependencies across intermediate outputs, and ground final responses in executed evidence. Small planners often generate plausible workflow graphs that fail under tool resolution, parameter validation, dependency tracking, or execution. We argue that this failure mode is poorly handled by small-corpus distillation. A few hundred teacher traces can teach workflow format, but rarely cover the recovery behavior needed to repair failed plans over changing tool catalogs. We introduce Evoflux, an inference-time evolutionary search method that treats compact tool use as the repair of executable tool workflows. It evolves typed workflow graphs through structured edits, execution feedback, adaptive intensity, meta-guided redesign, and diversity pruning. On held-out MCP-Bench tasks spanning live MCP servers and 250 tools, Evoflux raises execution feasibility from roughly 3% to 17-24% across small planners. In contrast, SFT and SFT+DPO on the same search-mined data match, underperform, or collapse below zero-shot performance; ReAct reaches higher peaks, but with higher variance and token cost. These results show that execution-grounded search is more reliable under scarce teacher-trace budgets.

ru.title: Эволюционный поиск для надёжного восстановления планов в компактных агентах
ru.desc: Evoflux — это метод эволюционного поиска, который позволяет компактным языковым моделям более надёжно выполнять рабочие процессы с инструментами путём восстановления неудачных планов во время инференции. В отличие от традиционного fine-tuning, который плохо справляется с восстановлением после ошибок валидации параметров и разрешения зависимостей, Evoflux использует структурированное редактирование графов рабочих процессов, обратную связь от исполнения и адаптивную интенсивность поиска. На бенчмарке MCP-Bench метод повышает вероятность успешного выполнения с 3% до 17-24%, превосходя методы supervised fine-tuning и reinforcement learning по надёжности при ограниченном количестве примеров обучения. Этот подход демонстрирует преимущество поиска, управляемого обратной связью от выполнения, для малых моделей с недостаточными объёмами обучающих данных.

en.title: Evoflux: Evolving Reliable Tool Workflows for Compact Language Models
en.desc: Evoflux is a method that enhances the reliability of compact language models when executing tool workflows by using evolutionary search to fix failed plans during inference. Traditional fine-tuning methods often struggle with the complexities of tool use, such as discovering tools and managing dependencies. Evoflux improves execution feasibility significantly, increasing success rates from about 3% to between 17% and 24% on various tasks. This approach demonstrates that using execution-grounded search is more effective than relying solely on small-corpus distillation for training models in dynamic environments.

zh.title: Evoflux：提升工具工作流执行可靠性的新方法
zh.desc: Evoflux是一种进化搜索方法，旨在提高紧凑语言模型在执行工具工作流时的可靠性。它通过修复失败的计划，显著改善了执行的可行性，超越了传统的微调方法。该方法通过结构化编辑、执行反馈和多样性修剪等技术，进化出可执行的工具工作流图。实验结果表明，Evoflux在多个任务中将执行可行性从约3%提高到17-24%。

papers.title: MuJoCo-Drones-Gym: A GPU-Accelerated Multi-Drone Simulator for Control and Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract A Gymnasium-compatible multi-drone simulation environment built on MuJoCo physics engine that supports flexible physics models, action interfaces, and observation spaces for reinforcement learning applications.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Robotic simulators are a cornerstone of modern research in aerial robotics, serving both as a vehicle for the development of new control algorithms and as the data source for training reinforcement learning (RL) policies. Yet, existing quadcopter learning environments often face a trade-off between physical fidelity, multi-agent support, and the throughput required by modern deep RL pipelines. In this paper, we present MuJoCo-Drones-Gym, an open-source Gymnasium-compatible multi-drone environment built on top of the MuJoCo physics engine. MuJoCo-Drones-Gym supports an arbitrary number of Bitcraze Crazyflie 2.x nano-quadcopters and exposes a modular API for selecting (i)~the physics model (rigid-body MuJoCo, explicit Python dynamics, or any subset of ground effect, blade drag, and inter-drone downwash), (ii)~the action interface (per-motor RPMs, collective normalized thrust, velocity setpoints, or PID waypoint commands), and (iii)~the observation space (kinematic state vectors, RGB / depth / segmentation cameras, or neighbourhood adjacency information). A PettingZoo ParallelEnv wrapper enables drop-in multi-agent reinforcement learning, while a suite of seven task environments, hover, velocity tracking, multi-drone hover, waypoint navigation, formation flight, gate racing, and a generic multi-agent template, demonstrates the breadth of the interface. We describe the environment design, the underlying physics and quadcopter dynamics, and illustrate its use through control and learning examples that mirror those of the closely related gym-pybullet-drones project, while taking advantage of MuJoCo's improved contact handling, rendering, and parallelizability.

ru.title: Гибкая платформа для обучения многодроновых систем на основе реалистичной физики
ru.desc: Авторы представляют MuJoCo-Drones-Gym — открытую симуляционную среду для обучения с подкреплением многодроновых систем на основе физического движка MuJoCo. Среда поддерживает гибкую конфигурацию физических моделей, интерфейсов управления и пространств наблюдений, что позволяет исследователям выбирать оптимальный баланс между точностью симуляции и вычислительной производительностью. Интеграция с фреймворком PettingZoo обеспечивает удобный API для мультиагентного обучения с подкреплением, а набор предзаданных задач демонстрирует универсальность платформы. Платформа превосходит существующие аналоги благодаря лучшей обработке контактов, возможностям рендеринга и параллелизации вычислений.

en.title: Empowering Multi-Drone Learning with MuJoCo-Drones-Gym
en.desc: This paper introduces MuJoCo-Drones-Gym, a versatile simulation environment for multi-drone applications compatible with Gymnasium, leveraging the MuJoCo physics engine. It allows researchers to customize various aspects such as physics models, action interfaces, and observation spaces, facilitating the development of reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms. The environment supports multiple drones, specifically the Bitcraze Crazyflie 2.x, and includes a range of tasks to test RL policies, from basic hovering to complex formation flying. By providing a modular API and enhanced physics capabilities, this platform aims to bridge the gap between high-fidelity simulations and the demands of modern deep RL training.

zh.title: 多无人机仿真环境的创新与应用
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一个基于MuJoCo物理引擎的多无人机仿真环境MuJoCo-Drones-Gym，兼容Gymnasium，支持灵活的物理模型、动作接口和观察空间，适用于强化学习应用。该环境能够支持任意数量的Bitcraze Crazyflie 2.x纳米四旋翼，并提供模块化API，用户可以选择不同的物理模型、动作接口和观察空间。通过PettingZoo ParallelEnv包装器，该环境支持多智能体强化学习，并提供七个任务环境示例，展示了接口的广泛性。我们还描述了环境设计、物理基础和四旋翼动力学，并通过控制和学习示例展示其应用。

papers.title: See What I See, Know What I Think: Dense Latent Communication Across Heterogeneous Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Heterogeneous multi-agent systems can effectively transfer knowledge through aligned KV-cache communication, achieving better performance than text-based methods with reduced computational costs.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Multi-agent systems communicate mostly through text, paying a lossy and expensive decode and re-encode cost. KV-cache communication is a promising alternative, yet most prior work is homogeneous, using duplicate copies of the same model, and avoids the central challenge of cross-model latent alignment; existing heterogeneous methods are also restrictive, typically assuming shared input and using transferred caches mainly for steering. We study a more fundamental question: can heterogeneous agents be aligned well enough to perform real "mind reading" and transfer both what one agent sees and how it thinks? Our information-structure analysis reveals a duality: context-aware transfer is driven by sparse reasoning signals, while context-unaware transfer, where the receiver sees no input, requires dense contextual knowledge preservation. Motivated by this, we propose dense alignment for heterogeneous KV-cache communication via a lightweight cross-model cache transformation and two-phase training: reconstruction followed by generation. Across all six directions of {Qwen3-4B, 8B, 14B} and six in-domain and out-of-domain benchmarks, our method outperforms prior heterogeneous baselines, matches or exceeds text communication in context-aware settings at roughly 2 to 3 times lower compute, and remains effective in context-unaware transfer where prior methods collapse.

ru.title: Телепатия моделей: выравненное общение через KV-cache для разнородных агентов
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод передачи знаний между разнородными мультиагентными системами через выравненное общение на уровне KV-cache вместо текстовых сообщений. Авторы анализируют информационную структуру и показывают, что эффективная передача требует разных подходов для случаев с контекстом (редкие сигналы рассуждений) и без контекста (плотное сохранение знаний). Предложенный метод использует лёгкую трансформацию кэша между моделями и двухфазное обучение (реконструкция и генерация) для выравнивания скрытых представлений. Эксперименты показывают, что метод превосходит текстовое общение в 2-3 раза по вычислительной эффективности при сохранении качества на различных бенчмарках.

en.title: Enhancing Knowledge Transfer in Multi-Agent Systems with KV-Cache Communication
en.desc: This paper explores how heterogeneous multi-agent systems can improve knowledge transfer using KV-cache communication instead of traditional text-based methods. The authors identify that previous approaches often rely on homogeneous models and do not effectively address the challenge of aligning different models' latent representations. They propose a novel method called dense alignment, which involves a lightweight transformation of caches and a two-phase training process to enhance communication between agents. Their results show that this approach significantly outperforms existing methods, achieving better performance with lower computational costs in various scenarios.

zh.title: 异构智能体的高效知识传递
zh.desc: 异构多智能体系统通过对齐的KV缓存通信有效地传递知识，表现优于基于文本的方法，并降低了计算成本。传统的多智能体系统主要通过文本进行交流，这样会产生高昂的解码和重编码成本。我们提出了一种新的方法，通过轻量级的跨模型缓存转换和两阶段训练，实现异构KV缓存通信的密集对齐。实验结果表明，我们的方法在多个基准测试中超越了之前的异构基线，并在上下文感知的设置中以大约2到3倍更低的计算成本匹配或超过了文本通信。

papers.title: Getting Better at Working With You: Compiling User Corrections into Runtime Enforcement for Coding Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract TRACE is a skill-layer pipeline that mines user corrections to create runtime checks, significantly reducing preference violations in interactive LLM agents.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Interactive LLM agents are becoming part of daily work, but they do not reliably become easier to work with over time: a correction remembered in one session may still be violated in the next. We study this gap between preference access and preference compliance. In tasks derived from anonymized real-user friction cases, Mem0 memory still leaves 57.5% of applicable preference checks violated. We introduce Test-time Rule Acquisition and Compiled Enforcement (TRACE), a drop-in skill-layer pipeline for coding-agent runtimes that mines user corrections, rewrites them as atomic rules, and compiles them into runtime checks that must pass before an agent completes future tasks. Unlike runtime checks written ahead of time by developers, TRACE skills come from the user's own chat corrections. We evaluate TRACE with simulated user-in-the-loop experiments on ClawArena coding-agent tasks and MemoryArena-derived memory-intensive tasks. On ClawArena, TRACE reduces held-out preference violation from 100.0% to 37.6% on in-distribution tasks and from 100.0% to 2.0% on out-of-distribution tasks. On MemoryArena-derived tasks, TRACE reduces in-distribution violation from 100.0% to 60.5% while matching or exceeding the strongest memory baseline on task pass. These results suggest that compiling corrections into runtime enforcement can address a repeated-friction failure mode that memory alone does not reliably solve, reducing the need for users to restate the same correction across future sessions. Experiment code is available at https://github.com/YujunZhou/TRACE_exp, and the deployable skill is available at https://github.com/YujunZhou/tellonce.

ru.title: Компилирование коррекций в runtime-проверки для надежного выполнения предпочтений пользователя
ru.desc: В статье представлена система TRACE для интерактивных LLM-агентов, которая автоматически преобразует исправления пользователя в проверяемые правила. Система анализирует корректировки пользователя, переписывает их как атомарные правила и компилирует в runtime-проверки, которые агент должен пройти перед выполнением задач. Эксперименты показывают, что TRACE значительно снижает нарушения предпочтений пользователя: на задачах ClawArena с 100% до 37.6% для распределённых примеров и до 2% для новых примеров. Предложенный подход решает проблему, которую традиционные системы памяти не могут надёжно решить, избавляя пользователей от необходимости повторять одно и то же исправление в разных сессиях.

en.title: Transforming User Corrections into Reliable Runtime Checks
en.desc: TRACE is a novel skill-layer pipeline designed to enhance interactive LLM agents by mining user corrections to create runtime checks. This approach addresses the issue of preference violations, where agents fail to remember user corrections across sessions. By converting user feedback into atomic rules, TRACE ensures that these rules are enforced during future tasks, significantly improving compliance with user preferences. The results demonstrate a substantial reduction in preference violations, showcasing TRACE's effectiveness in making LLM agents more reliable and user-friendly.

zh.title: TRACE：提升交互式代理的偏好合规性
zh.desc: TRACE是一种技能层管道，通过挖掘用户的修正来创建运行时检查，显著减少交互式大型语言模型（LLM）代理中的偏好违规。该方法将用户的修正转化为原子规则，并在未来任务完成之前进行编译检查。实验结果表明，TRACE在多个任务中有效降低了偏好违规的比例，尤其是在分布外任务中表现出色。TRACE的引入使得用户不必在每次会话中重复相同的修正，从而提高了交互式代理的可靠性和用户体验。

papers.title: ArogyaSutra: A Multi-Agent Framework for Multimodal Medical Reasoning in Indic Languages
papers.abstract: Abstract ArogyaBodha dataset and ArogyaSutra framework enhance multilingual medical reasoning in low-resource settings through diverse data integration and actor-critic multi-agent reasoning.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have shown promising reasoning capabilities in general domains, yet their performance remains limited in specialized settings such as healthcare, especially in multilingual and low-resource scenarios. This gap is critical in regions like rural India, where patients often express complex medical queries in native Indic languages and rely on multimodal inputs such as medical images. Existing English-centric MLLMs struggle to support such use cases, limiting equitable access to AI-driven healthcare assistance. To address this challenge, we introduce ArogyaBodha, a large-scale multilingual multimodal medical question-answer dataset constructed from eight heterogeneous sources, covering 31 body systems, six imaging modalities, and 21 clinical domains across English and seven major Indian languages. We further propose ArogyaSutra, an actor-critic-based multi-agent framework that integrates tool grounding with dual-memory mechanisms for step-wise, reasoning-aware decision making, and uses stored actor-critic simulation trajectories for distillation. Experiments show that our dataset and framework improve multilingual medical reasoning accuracy across all Indic languages, with ablations validating the contribution of each component. The source code and dataset are available at: https://iitp-cse.github.io/ ArogyaSutra/

ru.title: Многоязычное медицинское мышление для стран с ограниченными ресурсами через мультиагентные рассуждения
ru.desc: В работе представлен набор данных ArogyaBodha для медицинского AI, включающий вопросы и ответы на английском и семи индийских языках с медицинскими изображениями из восьми источников. Предложена система ArogyaSutra, использующая метод актёра-критика с несколькими агентами для пошагового медицинского рассуждения и принятия решений. Фреймворк интегрирует привязку инструментов и двойные механизмы памяти для улучшения качества рассуждений в условиях недостатка данных. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное повышение точности медицинского анализа на индийских языках благодаря предложенному подходу.

en.title: Empowering Multilingual Medical Reasoning in Low-Resource Settings
en.desc: The paper presents the ArogyaBodha dataset and ArogyaSutra framework aimed at improving multilingual medical reasoning in low-resource environments. It addresses the limitations of existing large language models (LLMs) in healthcare, particularly in rural India, where patients use native languages and multimodal inputs. The ArogyaBodha dataset is a comprehensive collection of medical questions and answers in multiple languages, covering various medical domains and imaging types. The ArogyaSutra framework employs an actor-critic multi-agent approach to enhance decision-making and reasoning capabilities, leading to improved accuracy in medical queries across different languages.

zh.title: 提升多语言医疗推理的创新解决方案
zh.desc: ArogyaBodha数据集和ArogyaSutra框架旨在通过多样化的数据整合和基于演员-评论家的多智能体推理，增强低资源环境下的多语言医疗推理能力。该数据集涵盖31个身体系统、6种成像模式和21个临床领域，支持英语和七种主要印度语言，解决了农村地区患者在使用母语表达复杂医疗问题时的困难。现有的以英语为中心的多模态大语言模型在医疗领域的表现有限，无法满足这些特定需求。通过实验，我们证明了该数据集和框架在所有印度语言中的多语言医疗推理准确性得到了提升。

papers.title: WEAVER, Better, Faster, Longer: An Effective World Model for Robotic Manipulation
papers.abstract: Abstract WEAVER is a multi-view world model architecture that achieves high fidelity, consistency, and efficiency in robotic manipulation tasks through flow-matching loss and demonstrates superior performance in policy evaluation, improvement, and test-time planning.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct The potential impacts of world models (WMs, i.e., learned simulators) on robotics are far-reaching -- policy evaluation, policy improvement, and test-time planning -- all with limited real-world interaction. To unlock these downstream capabilities, a WM needs to jointly satisfy three desiderata: (i) fidelity (i.e., producing simulated trajectories that correlate with reality), (ii) consistency (i.e., producing simulated trajectories that are coherent over long horizons), and (iii) efficiency (i.e., producing simulated trajectories quickly). We propose WEAVER (World Estimation Across Views for Embodied Reasoning): a WM architecture that simultaneously achieves all three desiderata, providing state-of-the-art results on robotic manipulation tasks. WEAVER is a multi-view WM trained to predict future latents and reward values via a flow-matching loss. We distill the key design decisions across model architecture, memory, and prediction objectives required to unlock the kinds of long-horizon dynamic manipulation tasks that have confounded prior world modeling approaches. We apply WEAVER in robotic hardware, demonstrating its effectiveness at policy evaluation (ρ=0.870 correlation with real-world success rate), policy improvement (real-world success rate improvement of 38% on top of the π_{0.5} robot foundation model), and test-time planning (real-world success rate improvement of 14% with a 5-10times speedup over prior WMs). WEAVER also demonstrates better performance than prior WMs when evaluated on out-of-distribution scenarios. Code, models, and videos at: https://arnavkj1995.github.io/WEAVER/ .

ru.title: Модель мира для робототехники через многовидовые представления
ru.desc: WEAVER — это архитектура модели мира на основе множественных представлений, которая обучается предсказывать будущие скрытые состояния и значения вознаграждения с помощью flow-matching потерь. Модель одновременно достигает высокую точность (соответствие симуляции реальности), последовательность (согласованность предсказаний на длинных горизонтах) и эффективность (быстрое генерирование траекторий). WEAVER применяется для оценки политик, их улучшения и планирования во время тестирования в задачах манипуляции роботами с минимальным взаимодействием с реальным миром. Результаты показывают значительное улучшение успешности выполнения задач на реальных роботах и лучшую обобщаемость на распределения данных, отличные от обучающих.

en.title: WEAVER: Revolutionizing Robotic Manipulation with Multi-View World Models
en.desc: WEAVER is a novel multi-view world model architecture designed for robotic manipulation tasks, focusing on high fidelity, consistency, and efficiency. It utilizes a flow-matching loss to predict future states and reward values, enabling effective policy evaluation and improvement with minimal real-world interaction. The architecture achieves superior performance by generating simulated trajectories that closely align with real-world outcomes, maintaining coherence over time, and doing so rapidly. By addressing the challenges of long-horizon dynamic manipulation, WEAVER outperforms previous models, demonstrating significant improvements in real-world success rates and planning efficiency.

zh.title: WEAVER：高效一致的多视角世界模型
zh.desc: WEAVER是一种多视角世界模型架构，能够在机器人操作任务中实现高保真度、一致性和高效率。它通过流匹配损失来预测未来的潜在状态和奖励值，从而在策略评估、改进和测试时规划中表现出色。WEAVER同时满足了保真度、一致性和效率三个关键要求，提供了最先进的机器人操作结果。该模型在实际硬件上应用，显示出在策略评估和改进方面的显著效果，且在处理分布外场景时表现优于以往的世界模型。

papers.title: Surflo: Consistent 3D Surface Flow Model with Global State
papers.abstract: Abstract Surflo compresses unposed RGB views into latent tokens and decodes 3D surface points through flow matching, enabling flexible resolution output and efficient processing compared to existing methods.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Geometry is invariant to viewpoint, which makes any collection of images a redundant encoding of a single 3D state. Existing feed-forward reconstruction models fail to exploit this: per-view methods emit overlapping, unaligned pointmaps that grow linearly with input count, while global-latent methods commit to a fixed, low-resolution output. We introduce Surflo, which compresses a variable number of unposed RGB views into K latent tokens-one global state-and decodes oriented 3D surface points by independently transporting them from noise onto the surface via flow matching. This frees the output from any fixed grid or token budget: the same latent yields from a few thousand to a million points in a single forward pass. To suppress the local inconsistencies inherent to independent per-point decoding, an inference-time guidance term correlates nearby points by injecting a photometric gradient during ODE integration. Surflo matches or surpasses feed-forward baselines on surface metrics, runs an order of magnitude faster than optimization-based methods that require hundreds of views, and is the only feed-forward approach to combine a global latent with arbitrary-resolution decoding.

ru.title: Гибкое восстановление 3D поверхностей с произвольным разрешением через flow matching
ru.desc: Surflo — это метод для реконструкции 3D поверхности из неотмеченных RGB изображений, который сжимает переменное количество изображений в K латентных токенов, представляющих глобальное состояние сцены. Декодирование 3D точек поверхности происходит через flow matching — независимое транспортирование каждой точки из шума на поверхность. Метод позволяет генерировать произвольное количество точек (от нескольких тысяч до миллиона) в одном forward pass, освобождая выход от фиксированного разрешения сетки. Для устранения локальной несогласованности при независимом декодировании точек используется guidance термин на этапе инференса, который коррелирует соседние точки через фотометрический градиент.

en.title: Surflo: Efficient 3D Surface Reconstruction from RGB Views
en.desc: Surflo is a novel machine learning method that compresses multiple unposed RGB images into a set of latent tokens, which represent a single 3D state. It decodes 3D surface points by using flow matching, allowing for flexible output resolutions and efficient processing. Unlike traditional methods that produce overlapping pointmaps or fixed-resolution outputs, Surflo can generate a variable number of points in a single pass, ranging from thousands to millions. Additionally, it incorporates a guidance term to enhance the consistency of the decoded points, achieving superior performance compared to existing feed-forward models.

zh.title: Surflo：高效灵活的3D重建新方法
zh.desc: Surflo是一种新型的机器学习方法，它将未定姿态的RGB视图压缩成潜在标记，并通过流匹配解码3D表面点。这种方法能够灵活输出不同分辨率的结果，并且处理效率高于现有方法。Surflo利用全局状态的潜在标记，避免了传统方法中由于视图重叠而导致的点图不对齐问题。通过在推理时引入光度梯度，Surflo能够有效抑制局部不一致性，提供更高质量的3D重建。

papers.title: IDEAL: In-DEpth ALignment Makes A Discrete Representation AutoEncoder
papers.abstract: Abstract Representation autoencoders using deep learning frameworks can improve image reconstruction quality by combining shallow and deep visual feature representations for better semantic richness and visual fidelity.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Built on pretrained vision foundation models (VFMs), representation autoencoders (RAEs) have recently emerged as a promising approach for constructing semantically rich latent spaces for image generation. However, their reconstruction quality often remains suboptimal, largely because deep VFM representations do not preserve sufficient fine-grained visual detail. This limitation becomes even more severe after discretization, where missing low-level information is difficult to recover. In fact, we observe that shallow VFM features retain considerably richer local appearance and structural detail, which complements the high-level semantics carried by deep features used in existing RAEs. Motivated by this complementary property, we propose Ideal, an In-depth Alignment framework for discrete representation autoencoding. By jointly aligning quantized tokens with both shallow and deep VFM features, Ideal enables the resulting discrete visual tokens to preserve both visual fidelity and rich semantics. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Ideal yields superior reconstruction performance, achieving 0.61 rFID on ImageNet and outperforming the previous best method by 0.28. When used for autoregressive image generation, Ideal further produces a gFID of 1.89, establishing a new state of the art for autoregressive image generation.
affiliations: Institute of Trustworthy Embodied AI, Fudan University

ru.title: Слияние неглубоких и глубоких признаков для совершенной дискретной кодировки изображений
ru.desc: В этой работе предлагается новый метод дискретного автоэнкодера представлений (RAE), который улучшает качество реконструкции изображений благодаря совместному использованию признаков из неглубоких и глубоких слоёв предобученных визуальных моделей. Авторы замечают, что неглубокие слои сохраняют больше локальных деталей и структурной информации, а глубокие слои — высокоуровневую семантику, что дополняет друг друга. Предложенный фреймворк Ideal выравнивает квантованные токены как с поверхностными, так и с глубокими признаками, что позволяет дискретным визуальным токенам сохранять одновременно визуальную точность и богатую семантику. Экспериментальные результаты показывают новое состояние искусства в задачах реконструкции и автогрессивной генерации изображений.

en.title: Enhancing Image Reconstruction with Ideal Framework
en.desc: This paper introduces Ideal, a new framework for representation autoencoders that enhances image reconstruction quality by integrating both shallow and deep visual feature representations. The authors highlight that while deep visual foundation models (VFMs) provide high-level semantic information, they often lack fine-grained details necessary for high-quality image generation. By aligning quantized tokens with both shallow and deep features, Ideal effectively captures both visual fidelity and rich semantics. Experimental results show that Ideal significantly improves reconstruction performance and sets a new benchmark in autoregressive image generation.

zh.title: 理想框架：提升图像重建质量的创新之路
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种名为Ideal的框架，用于改进图像重建质量。通过结合浅层和深层视觉特征，Ideal能够在离散表示自编码中实现更好的语义丰富性和视觉保真度。研究表明，浅层特征保留了更丰富的局部细节，这与深层特征的高层语义互补。实验结果显示，Ideal在ImageNet上实现了0.61的重建性能，超越了之前的最佳方法。

papers.title: Revisiting Articulated Parts Perception in Robot Manipulation
papers.abstract: Abstract A new geometric representation called Geometric Primary Structure (GPS) is introduced for articulated parts perception, enabling efficient data collection through VR annotation and achieving high manipulation success rates without fine-tuning.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We are surrounded by various objects with movable, articulated parts, e.g., box, handle, door. An accurate and generalizable perception of articulated parts is essential to enhance robotic manipulation capabilities. Building on this need, recent efforts in articulated parts perception have followed two main directions: One line of work uses pose-based representation, which requires high manual cost; in parallel, affordance-based methods extract future object motion from point tracking without additional manual efforts, but suffer from low-quality data. In this paper, we propose a new representation of articulated parts, Geometric Primary Structure (GPS), an abstraction of the part geometry structure to balance scalability and quality. For efficient and scalable data collection, GPS is integrated with a portable Virtual Reality (VR) device and requires only one minute to annotate one object sequence. This direct human annotation provides higher quality than the estimated affordance. With this efficient VR-GPS system, we collect 41K frames for 234 objects across six part classes, and train a generalizable GPS model with a single RGB-D object image as input. For object manipulation, we deploy a heuristic policy based on GPS prediction. Without any in-domain fine-tuning, our method achieves an 73% success rate, covering 270 initial states for 9 objects. Our code, data and reusable tool are available at https://enlighten0707.github.io/gps.

ru.title: Геометрическая структура для восприятия сочленённых частей в робототехнике
ru.desc: Авторы представляют новое геометрическое представление Geometric Primary Structure (GPS) для восприятия сочленённых частей объектов, которое балансирует между масштабируемостью и качеством данных. Для эффективного сбора обучающих данных они разработали систему на основе VR-устройства, позволяющую аннотировать объект за одну минуту, что обеспечивает более высокое качество по сравнению с методами на основе аффордансов. На собранном датасете из 41K кадров 234 объектов обучена обобщающая модель, которая работает с одного RGB-D изображения объекта. Развёрнутая система достигает 73% успеха в манипуляции объектами без дополнительной подстройки модели на целевом домене.

en.title: Revolutionizing Object Manipulation with Geometric Primary Structure
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method called Geometric Primary Structure (GPS) for understanding objects with movable parts, which is important for improving how robots handle these objects. GPS allows for quick and efficient data collection using Virtual Reality (VR) for annotation, requiring only a minute to label each object sequence. The method combines the benefits of high-quality human input with the scalability of automated systems, resulting in a dataset of 41,000 frames for training. The GPS model can predict object manipulation tasks effectively, achieving a 73% success rate without needing additional fine-tuning.

zh.title: 几何主结构：提升机器人操作能力的新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的几何表示方法，称为几何主结构（GPS），用于感知可动部件。通过虚拟现实（VR）注释，GPS能够高效地收集数据，并在不进行微调的情况下实现高达73%的操作成功率。与传统的基于姿态的表示方法相比，GPS在可扩展性和数据质量之间取得了良好的平衡。我们使用GPS模型进行物体操作，展示了其在多种初始状态下的有效性。

papers.title: The Cold-Start Safety Gap in LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Tool-calling language model agents exhibit improved safety after initial interactions, with a systematic benchmark demonstrating enhanced security through prior task completion.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Are tool-calling LLM agents equally safe throughout a conversation? We discover they are not: agents are most vulnerable at the very start of a session and become substantially safer after a few regular agentic tasks -- a phenomenon we term the cold-start safety gap. To study this systematically, we introduce Safety Over Depth for Agents (SODA), a benchmark that controls how many regular agentic tasks the agent completes before encountering a safety threat, supporting up to 20 preceding tasks. Evaluating 7 models from 4 families, safety improves by 9--52% as the number of preceding regular agentic tasks increases from zero to twenty. Representation analysis confirms that model hidden states gradually shift toward a safety-aligned region as more preceding tasks are present. By systematically studying which part of the preceding conversation matters most, we find that the regular agentic tasks themselves are the primary driver of safety, while the agent's own prior responses have less effect on safety but are essential for preserving later utility. This conclusion is further supported by evaluation on open-source safety benchmarks (AgentHarm, Agent Safety Bench) and utility benchmarks (BFCL, API-Bank), confirming that warming up the agent with regular agentic tasks before deployment makes it safer and preserves full capability. Based on these findings, we recommend a simple deployment strategy: having the agent complete a few regular agentic tasks before possible exposure to safety-critical requests mitigates the cold-start safety gap. Our code is available at https://github.com/Trustworthy-ML-Lab/Agent-Cold-Start-Safety-Gap

ru.title: Разминка агента улучшает его безопасность: преодоление проблемы холодного старта
ru.desc: В этой работе исследователи обнаружили, что агенты на основе больших языковых моделей (LLM) наиболее уязвимы в начале сессии и становятся значительно безопаснее после выполнения нескольких обычных задач - явление, названное "проблемой безопасности холодного старта". Для систематического изучения этого феномена авторы разработали бенчмарк SODA (Safety Over Depth for Agents), который контролирует количество предварительных задач перед воздействием угроз безопасности. Анализ скрытых состояний модели показал, что представления постепенно смещаются в сторону выровненного по безопасности региона по мере выполнения большего числа предварительных задач. На основе полученных результатов авторы рекомендуют простую стратегию развёртывания: предварительное выполнение агентом нескольких обычных задач перед возможным воздействием критичных по безопасности запросов значительно снижает риск.

en.title: Boosting Agent Safety Through Task Warm-Up
en.desc: This paper investigates the safety of tool-calling language model (LLM) agents during conversations, revealing that they are most vulnerable at the beginning of a session. The authors introduce a benchmark called Safety Over Depth for Agents (SODA) to measure how safety improves as agents complete regular tasks before facing safety threats. Their findings show that safety increases by 9-52% as agents perform more tasks, indicating that these tasks help align the model's hidden states with safer outcomes. The study concludes that warming up agents with regular tasks before critical requests enhances their safety while maintaining their performance capabilities.

zh.title: 提升代理安全性的简单策略
zh.desc: 本文研究了工具调用语言模型代理在初始交互后的安全性提升。我们发现，代理在会话开始时最脆弱，经过几次常规任务后安全性显著提高，这一现象被称为冷启动安全差距。通过引入安全深度基准（SODA），我们系统地评估了代理在完成常规任务后的安全性变化，结果显示安全性随着任务数量的增加而提升。我们的研究表明，完成常规任务是提高安全性的主要因素，而代理自身的先前响应对安全性的影响较小，但对保持后续效用至关重要。

papers.title: ToolSense: A Diagnostic Framework for Auditing Parametric Tool Knowledge in LLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract Parametric tool retrieval models show reduced performance and understanding when evaluated with realistic ambiguous queries compared to standard benchmarks, revealing a dissociation between knowledge retrieval and true tool comprehension.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large language models deployed as agents over large tool catalogs face a critical tool-retrieval bottleneck. As embedding-based retrieval approaches rely on compact encoders that may under-capture specialized tool semantics, parametric tool retrieval addresses this by encoding each tool as a virtual token appended to the LLM vocabulary, fine-tuned in two stages (memorization then retrieval SFT) to use the LLM as a retriever, achieving strong performance on standard ToolBench retrieval benchmarks. Yet these benchmarks use verbose, fully-specified queries, and their evaluation applies constrained decoding that restricts outputs to valid token paths, neither reveals whether the model actually understands its tools. We introduce ToolSense, an open-source LLM-powered diagnostic framework that takes any tool catalog as input and automatically generates three benchmarks: a Realistic Retrieval Benchmark (RRB) with queries at three ambiguity tiers, an MCQ probing benchmark, and a QA probing benchmark. Applying ToolSense to ToolBench (~47k tools) and evaluating five parametric model training configurations reveals a knowledge-retrieval dissociation: on RRB queries, several configurations collapse by ~50-64 percentage points compared to fully-specified ToolBench benchmarks, falling below the embedding-model baseline. Additionally, despite strong retrieval performance, some models score near-random on factual probes, suggesting a knowledge-retrieval dissociation. We open-source the ToolSense framework and the ToolBench diagnostic benchmarks at https://github.com/SAP/toolsense.

ru.title: Разрыв между извлечением и пониманием: диагностика параметрических моделей получения инструментов
ru.desc: Исследование показывает, что параметрические модели получения инструментов демонстрируют значительное снижение производительности при работе с реалистичными неоднозначными запросами по сравнению со стандартными бенчмарками. Авторы предлагают ToolSense — диагностический фреймворк на основе LLM, который автоматически генерирует три набора тестов для оценки истинного понимания инструментов моделью. Применение ToolSense к ToolBench выявило критическую рассогласованность между способностью к извлечению информации и реальным пониманием: некоторые конфигурации показывают падение производительности на 50-64 процентных пункта при работе с амбигуозными запросами. Открытый фреймворк ToolSense предоставляет сообществу надежный способ диагностики реальных возможностей моделей в задачах получения инструментов.

en.title: Bridging the Gap: Understanding Tool Retrieval Beyond Standard Benchmarks
en.desc: This paper discusses the limitations of parametric tool retrieval models when faced with ambiguous queries, highlighting a gap between knowledge retrieval and true understanding of tools. The authors introduce ToolSense, a framework that generates realistic benchmarks to evaluate tool retrieval performance under varying levels of query ambiguity. They find that many models perform significantly worse on these realistic queries compared to standard benchmarks, indicating a knowledge-retrieval dissociation. The study emphasizes the need for better evaluation methods to truly assess the comprehension capabilities of large language models in tool retrieval tasks.

zh.title: 工具检索与理解的脱节问题
zh.desc: 本文探讨了参数化工具检索模型在处理模糊查询时的性能下降，揭示了知识检索与工具理解之间的脱节。研究提出了一种名为ToolSense的开源框架，能够自动生成多种基准测试，以评估工具检索的真实能力。通过对ToolBench的评估，发现一些模型在模糊查询上的表现显著低于标准基准，显示出知识检索的不足。尽管某些模型在检索性能上表现良好，但在事实探测上却接近随机，进一步证明了知识与检索之间的脱节。

papers.title: A Stationary (and Therefore Compatible) Representation is All You Need
papers.abstract: Abstract Stationary representations learned through d-Simplex fixed classifiers ensure model compatibility during sequential fine-tuning and updates, enabling continuous retrieval services without reprocessing.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Learning compatible representations aims to learn feature representations that can be used interchangeably over time whenever a model undergoes updates. In this paper, we demonstrate that stationary representations learned by d-Simplex fixed classifiers imply compatibility as in its formal definition. This result establishes a foundation for future works and can be directly exploited in practical learning scenarios. We address the challenge of learning compatibility using d-Simplex fixed classifiers when the model is sequentially fine-tuned. Learning according to a d-Simplex fixed classifier with the cross-entropy loss aligns feature distributions at the first-order statistics. Consequently, it may not fully capture higher-order dependencies in the representation between model updates. To address this issue, we demonstrate that training the model using a d-Simplex fixed classifier through a convex combination of the cross-entropy loss and a contrastive loss not only captures higher-order dependencies, but is also equivalent to learning with the cross-entropy under the compatibility constraints. We confirm our findings with extensive experiments also considering a new scenario where a pre-trained model is sequentially fine-tuned and occasionally replaced with an improved model. We show that stationary representations enable uninterrupted retrieval services (without reprocessing gallery images) while improving performance during model updates and replacements, achieving state-of-the-art. Code at https://github.com/miccunifi/iamcl2r.
affiliations: Media Integration and Communication Center (MICC), Dipartimento di Ingegneria dell'Informazione, Università degli Studi di Firenze

ru.title: Неподвижные представления для совместимых моделей при постоянной адаптации
ru.desc: В статье предложен метод обучения стационарных представлений с использованием d-Simplex фиксированных классификаторов, который обеспечивает совместимость моделей при последовательной тонкой настройке. Авторы показывают, что комбинирование кросс-энтропийной потери с контрастивной потерей позволяет уловить зависимости высшего порядка в представлениях между обновлениями модели. Предложенный подход гарантирует непрерывную работу систем поиска и извлечения информации без переобработки данных при обновлении или замене модели. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют эффективность метода при работе с предварительно обученными моделями, подвергающимися последовательной адаптации.

en.title: Achieving Continuous Model Compatibility with Stationary Representations
en.desc: This paper introduces a method for learning stationary representations using d-Simplex fixed classifiers, which ensures that models remain compatible during sequential updates. The approach allows for continuous retrieval services without the need to reprocess data, making it efficient for real-time applications. By combining cross-entropy loss with contrastive loss, the method captures both first-order and higher-order dependencies in feature representations. The results demonstrate improved performance during model updates, establishing a foundation for future research in compatible learning scenarios.

zh.title: 静态表示：模型更新中的兼容性保障
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种通过d-Simplex固定分类器学习的静态表示，确保模型在顺序微调和更新过程中的兼容性。这种表示可以在模型更新时无缝使用，避免了重新处理数据的需求。我们展示了d-Simplex固定分类器学习的静态表示如何符合兼容性的正式定义，并为未来的研究奠定基础。通过结合交叉熵损失和对比损失的训练方法，我们不仅捕捉了高阶依赖关系，还在兼容性约束下实现了与交叉熵学习的等效性。

papers.title: PianoKontext: Expressive Performance Rendering from Deadpan Context
papers.abstract: Abstract PianoKontext generates variable-length piano performances by aligning MIDI scores with audio in latent space using DTW and DiT blocks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Expressive performance rendering (EPR) aims to generate realistic performances constrained on sequences of notes. However, flow matching audio editing models manipulate only synchronized music samples of the same duration, limiting their understanding of expressive timing. We introduce PianoKontext, a flow matching rendering model for classical piano music that generates variable-length performances in the latent space of a pretrained Music2Latent model. We synthesize MIDI scores into deadpan audio and employ Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) in the latent space to construct paired data for training. The aligned embeddings are concatenated in DiT blocks, allowing for a simple and effective learning of the dependencies between the score and performances. Audio samples are available at our demo page: https://realfolkcode.github.io/pianokontext_demo/.

ru.title: Музыка без ограничений: синтез выразительного пианино через выравнивание в скрытом пространстве
ru.desc: PianoKontext — это модель, которая генерирует пианические исполнения переменной длины, выравнивая MIDI-партитуры с аудио в скрытом пространстве. Авторы используют Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) для построения обучающих пар данных и трансформер блоки (DiT) для обучения зависимостям между нотами и их выразительным исполнением. Модель работает в латентном пространстве предварительно обученной модели Music2Latent, что позволяет обрабатывать последовательности разной длительности. Метод решает проблему выразительного синтеза музыки, позволяя модели понять естественные вариации во времени исполнения классической пианино музыки.

en.title: Transforming MIDI to Expressive Piano Performances
en.desc: PianoKontext is a machine learning model designed to create variable-length piano performances by aligning MIDI scores with audio in a latent space. It utilizes Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) to match the timing of notes in the MIDI with the audio, allowing for more expressive and realistic performances. The model leverages DiT blocks to learn the relationships between the musical score and the generated audio, enhancing its ability to render expressive performances. This approach overcomes limitations of previous models that only worked with synchronized audio samples of the same length.

zh.title: 生成可变长度钢琴表演的创新模型
zh.desc: PianoKontext 是一种生成可变长度钢琴表演的模型，它通过在潜在空间中对齐 MIDI 乐谱和音频来实现。该模型使用动态时间规整（DTW）和 DiT 块来构建训练数据，从而有效学习乐谱与表演之间的依赖关系。与传统的音频编辑模型不同，PianoKontext 能够处理不同长度的音乐片段，提升了对表现性节奏的理解。通过预训练的 Music2Latent 模型，PianoKontext 能够合成出更真实的钢琴表演。

papers.title: WebChallenger: A Reliable and Efficient Generalist Web Agent
papers.abstract: Abstract WebChallenger presents a web agent framework that improves autonomous navigation through structured page representation and cognitive-inspired mechanisms, achieving high performance with open-weight models.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Autonomous web navigation remains challenging for LLM agents, and the strongest generalist systems rely on proprietary reasoning models whose inference cost is prohibitive for the repetitive tasks where such agents would be most useful. We argue this gap stems not from insufficient model capability but from agent architectures that fail to replicate three human cognitive advantages: selective attention to relevant page regions, persistent memory of website structure, and procedural fluency with common interaction patterns. We introduce WebChallenger, a web agent framework that addresses each gap through architecture design rather than model scale, built around PageMem: a structured page representation deterministically constructed from the DOM that exposes each page as a hierarchy of semantic sections with short summaries. On this shared substrate we build three mechanisms that mirror the three cognitive advantages: a divide-and-conquer observation pipeline that lets the agent skim section summaries and extract details only from task-relevant regions; a lightweight exploration and memory system that traverses each website once to build a reusable map of pages and element behaviors; and compound action workflows that collapse common multi-step interactions into single agent actions, handling partial state changes automatically. Because all three operate over PageMem, the framework generalizes across websites without site-specific adapters. Using off-the-shelf open-weight models without fine-tuning, our system achieves 56.3% on WebArena, 48.7% on VisualWebArena, 51.0% on Online-Mind2Web, and 70.9% on WorkArena, approaching frontier proprietary systems at a fraction of the cost. Our code is released at https://github.com/jayoohwang1/webchallenger

ru.title: Умная навигация по веб-сайтам без больших моделей — только архитектура имеет значение
ru.desc: WebChallenger — это фреймворк для веб-агентов на основе больших языковых моделей, который улучшает автономную навигацию по интернету через структурированное представление страниц и когнитивные механизмы. Авторы предлагают архитектурное решение, основанное на PageMem — структурированном представлении DOM-дерева с семантическими секциями и их описаниями, которое позволяет агенту эффективнее работать с веб-страницами. Фреймворк реализует три ключевых механизма: избирательное внимание к релевантным регионам страницы, устойчивую память о структуре сайта и автоматизацию типичных многошаговых взаимодействий через составные действия. Система достигает высоких результатов на нескольких бенчмарках, используя открытые модели без дополнительного обучения, конкурируя с закрытыми коммерческими системами при значительно меньших затратах на вычисления.

en.title: Empowering Web Agents with Human-like Cognition
en.desc: WebChallenger is a web agent framework designed to enhance autonomous navigation by utilizing structured page representations and cognitive-inspired mechanisms. It addresses the limitations of existing agent architectures by incorporating features that mimic human cognitive advantages, such as selective attention, persistent memory, and procedural fluency. The framework employs a structured page representation called PageMem, which organizes web pages into a hierarchy of semantic sections, allowing agents to efficiently navigate and interact with content. By leveraging open-weight models without the need for fine-tuning, WebChallenger achieves competitive performance on various benchmarks, demonstrating its effectiveness and cost-efficiency compared to proprietary systems.

zh.title: WebChallenger：提升自主导航的智能框架
zh.desc: WebChallenger是一个网络代理框架，通过结构化页面表示和认知启发机制来改善自主导航。该框架解决了现有代理架构无法复制人类认知优势的问题，包括选择性注意、持久记忆和程序流畅性。WebChallenger使用PageMem构建页面的层次语义结构，并通过三种机制来提升导航效率。该系统在多个测试平台上表现出色，使用开放权重模型而无需微调，成本大幅降低。

papers.title: Flash-GMM: A Memory-Efficient Kernel for Scalable Soft Clustering
papers.abstract: Abstract Flash-GMM introduces an efficient fused Triton kernel for Gaussian Mixture Models that achieves significant speedup and enables processing much larger datasets on a single GPU.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We present Flash-GMM, a fused Triton kernel for efficient computation of Gaussian Mixture Models (GMMs) over large-scale data in a single GPU pass. By eliminating the need to materialize the full responsibility matrix in GPU memory, Flash-GMM achieves a 20times speedup over existing implementations and enables training on datasets more than 100times larger than previously feasible on one device. To demonstrate its impact, we integrate Flash-GMM into the IVF coarse quantizer for approximate nearest-neighbor (ANN) search. We show that soft GMM clustering is now a viable drop-in replacement for k-means, and that GMM responsibilities can be leveraged to assign border vectors to multiple clusters. Our approach reaches fixed recall targets with up to 1.7times fewer distance computations, or equivalently, yields +2--12 recall@10 at matched computational cost. We release the kernel as an open-source project.

ru.title: Ускорение гауссовых смесей через эффективные GPU ядра
ru.desc: Flash-GMM представляет оптимизированное ядро Triton для эффективного обучения гауссовых смесей (GMM) на больших датасетах с использованием одного GPU. Основное улучшение достигается за счет исключения необходимости сохранения полной матрицы ответственности в памяти видеокарты, что дает 20-кратное ускорение и позволяет обрабатывать датасеты в 100 раз больше, чем раньше. Авторы применили Flash-GMM в качестве замены k-means в коарсном квантизаторе IVF для приблизительного поиска ближайших соседей (ANN), используя вероятности принадлежности для назначения векторов нескольким кластерам одновременно. Предложенный подход снижает количество вычислений расстояний на 30% при достижении одинаковых метрик качества и распространяется как открытый исходный код.

en.title: Speeding Up GMMs: Flash-GMM Revolutionizes Large-Scale Data Processing
en.desc: Flash-GMM is a new method that improves the efficiency of Gaussian Mixture Models (GMMs) by using a fused Triton kernel, allowing for faster computations on large datasets with a single GPU. It avoids the need to store the entire responsibility matrix in GPU memory, resulting in a remarkable 20 times speedup compared to previous methods. This advancement enables the processing of datasets that are over 100 times larger than what was previously possible on one device. Additionally, Flash-GMM can be integrated into approximate nearest-neighbor search, making soft GMM clustering a practical alternative to k-means clustering, while also reducing the number of distance calculations needed for similar recall performance.

zh.title: Flash-GMM：高效处理大规模数据的革命性方法
zh.desc: Flash-GMM是一种高效的融合Triton内核，专为高斯混合模型（GMM）设计，能够在单个GPU上处理大规模数据。通过消除在GPU内存中生成完整责任矩阵的需求，Flash-GMM实现了比现有实现快20倍的速度提升，并使得在单个设备上训练的数据集规模比以往大100倍。该方法还被集成到IVF粗量化器中，用于近似最近邻（ANN）搜索，展示了软GMM聚类可以作为k-means的有效替代方案。我们的研究表明，GMM的责任可以用于将边界向量分配给多个聚类，从而在计算成本相同的情况下，达到更高的召回率。

papers.title: Leveraging Morphology for Historical Script Metrological Analysis
papers.abstract: Abstract A transformer-based architecture with prototype learning enables scalable paleographic measurements from historical documents using only line-level transcriptions, demonstrating its effectiveness on a 160-page codex with minimal training data requirements.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Advances in handwritten text recognition have enabled large-scale transcription of historical documents, but still provide limited access to interpretable visual measurements for paleography, the study of historical scripts. In this paper, our main insight is that morphological script analysis, in particular the capacity to learn character prototypes from line-level transcriptions, enables the definition of scalable, meaningful, and stable paleographic measurements. More precisely, we leverage a transformer-based detection architecture together with a prototype-based line reconstruction module to learn prototypical characters and their occurrence, deformation, and positioning.   Our contributions are twofold. First, we introduce a deep architecture and learning methodology that enables efficient character modeling with only line-level transcription supervision, significantly improving over the Learnable Typewriter baseline and enabling accurate character bounding box prediction, unlocking its potential for paleographic measurements. Second, we introduce and demonstrate the paleographical relevance of automatic measurements enabled by our architecture for characters, bi-grams, and spaces between graphical units. For this demonstration, we extend the annotations of the codex Paris, BnF, fr. 2813, commissioned in the late fourteenth century by Charles V and copied by four hands, to 160 pages. We visualize our measurements over these pages, showing how they enable us not only to differentiate graphical profiles, but also to discover and analyze subtle variations. This case study outlines the scalability of our approach and its frugality in terms of required training data, since a single column of text is sufficient to compute our measurements on each of the 160 pages.   Data and code are publicly available at: https://malamatenia.github.io/morphology4metrology-analysis.
affiliations: Institut de Recherche et dHistoire des Textes, Paris, Île-de-France, France
LIGM, École des Ponts et Chaussées, IP Paris, CNRS, France

ru.title: Прототипное обучение для измерения палеографических признаков исторических манускриптов
ru.desc: Статья описывает трансформер-based архитектуру с прототипным обучением для анализа исторических рукописей, которая позволяет извлекать палеографические измерения символов только из построчных транскрипций. Авторы используют детектор на основе трансформеров и модуль реконструкции строк для изучения типичных форм символов и их деформаций. Методология требует минимально аннотированных данных - достаточно одной колонки текста для обработки всей страницы. Подход продемонстрирован на 160 страницах средневекового кодекса, где система успешно выявила различия между почерками разных писцов и выполнила точные измерения межсимвольных расстояний.

en.title: Transforming Paleography with Prototype Learning
en.desc: This paper presents a transformer-based architecture that utilizes prototype learning to enhance paleographic measurements from historical documents. By focusing on line-level transcriptions, the model efficiently learns character prototypes, allowing for scalable and interpretable analysis of historical scripts. The authors demonstrate that their approach significantly improves character bounding box predictions and enables automatic measurements of characters and their relationships. This method requires minimal training data, showcasing its effectiveness on a 160-page codex with just a single column of text for analysis.

zh.title: 基于变换器的古文字测量新方法
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种基于变换器架构的原型学习方法，能够从历史文献中进行可扩展的古文字测量，仅需行级转录数据。我们通过学习字符原型，定义了有意义且稳定的古文字测量，显著提高了字符建模的效率。该方法在160页的手稿上进行了验证，展示了其在古文字学中的应用潜力。我们的研究表明，使用单列文本即可计算出所需的测量数据，极大地减少了训练数据的需求。

papers.title: On the Limits of LLM Adaptability: Impact of Model-Internalized Priors on Annotation Task Performance
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models exhibit limited ability to correct zero-shot errors through prompting, with model performance more strongly linked to definition-specific familiarity than text-level memorization metrics.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used for zero-shot annotation and LLM-as-a-judge tasks, yet their reliability hinges on how model-internalized priors interact with user-provided instructions. We investigate three dimensions of this interaction: (1) how an LLM's familiarity with data and task definitions affects performance, (2) the extent to which additional information in prompts can correct zero-shot errors ("decision stickiness"), and (3) model susceptibility to misaligned task definitions. Through experiments on toxicity detection across diverse datasets (spanning social media, gaming, news, and forums) using both dense and mixture-of-experts models, we find that nearly two-thirds of zero-shot errors are resistant to correction, with an overall rescue rate (fraction of initial errors corrected by prompting) of only 34.8%. High-confidence errors prove especially resistant to correction. When given misaligned definitions, LLMs follow them while maintaining confidence levels unchanged from the aligned condition. Crucially, we introduce Definition-Specific Familiarity (DSF), which measures alignment between a model's internal concept and the task definition. After controlling for dataset-level confounds, DSF shows a positive association with model performance (partial r = +0.41), while three distinct memorization metrics (ROUGE-L, BERTScore, and embedding cosine similarity) all fail to show a positive association. These findings show the limitations of prompt-based correction in annotation tasks, highlighting the importance of definition alignment over text-level memorization.

ru.title: Внутреннее согласие модели с определениями важнее, чем количество информации в промптах
ru.desc: Исследование показывает, что большие языковые модели плохо исправляют ошибки через промпты, так как две трети ошибок устойчивы к коррекции независимо от дополнительной информации. Авторы обнаружили, что производительность моделей зависит не от заучивания текста, а от того, насколько хорошо внутренние представления модели согласованы с определениями задачи. Введённая метрика Definition-Specific Familiarity показывает сильную корреляцию с точностью (r = +0.41), в то время как традиционные метрики меморизации (ROUGE-L, BERTScore) не предсказывают производительность. Исследование проводилось на задаче детекции токсичности с использованием различных архитектур моделей и демонстрирует критическую важность выравнивания определений вместо простого увеличения информации в промптах.

en.title: Aligning Definitions for Better Model Performance
en.desc: This paper explores the limitations of large language models (LLMs) in correcting errors during zero-shot tasks, emphasizing that their performance is more influenced by familiarity with specific definitions than by general text memorization. The authors investigate how well LLMs can adapt to user instructions and correct mistakes, revealing that a significant portion of errors remains unchanged despite additional prompting. They introduce the concept of Definition-Specific Familiarity (DSF), which correlates positively with model performance, indicating that alignment between the model's understanding and task definitions is crucial. The study concludes that relying on prompts for error correction in annotation tasks is often ineffective, underscoring the need for better alignment of definitions.

zh.title: 定义一致性是提升模型表现的关键
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了大型语言模型在零样本任务中的表现，特别是它们在纠正错误时的局限性。研究发现，模型的表现与其对特定定义的熟悉程度关系更大，而不是与文本级的记忆能力相关。通过对多种数据集的实验，发现近三分之二的零样本错误难以纠正，且高置信度的错误尤其顽固。论文引入了定义特定熟悉度（DSF）这一概念，强调了任务定义的一致性对模型表现的重要性。
